title: Utvecklare till Ung fritid och mötesplatser
description:
Vill du vara med och påverka för barn och ungdomar på våra fritidsgårdar och mötesplatser? Vi söker en utvecklare som vill vara med och påverka verksamheten.
  
  

Vårt uppdrag är att skapa möjligheter till en meningsfull fritid för våra besökare, där de får känna inflytande och delaktighet över sin fritid och forumet blir en arena för personlig och social utveckling. Vi arbetar främjande för att skapa en god folkhälsa, skapa goda förutsättningar och livsvillkor för våra besökare.
  
  

1 plats(er).
  
  

ARBETSUPPGIFTER
  
I rollen som utvecklare är din uppgift att bidra till enhetens utveckling på ledningsgruppsnivå samt tillsammans med arbetsgrupperna på enheten. Du genomför långsiktiga planer och strategier som bidrar till en meningsfull fritid för främst barn och unga men även för övriga besökare på mötesplatsen. Utgångspunkten i arbetet sker utifrån inflytande av våra barn och ungdomar. Vi samverkar med bland annat skola, socialtjänst och polis, där vi tillsammans identifierar behov och främjande insatser för våra barn och ungdomar.
  
  

I rollen jobbar du också med att utveckla kompetensen på enheten genom exempelvis utbildningar och workshops.
  
  

KVALIFIKATIONER
  
Vi är intresserade av dig som har en högskoleexamen med inriktning som exempelvis socionom, beteendevetenskap, folkhälsovetare, lärare eller annan utbildning som vi anser likvärdig. Du har erfarenhet av att jobba med ungdomar, gärna i en öppen ungdomsverksamhet. Har du jobbat med utveckling är det bra. Det är också positivt om du har jobbat i socialt utsatta områden och är van att samverka med andra aktörer. Ibland kör du bil i tjänsten, det är därför positivt med ett B-körkort.
  
  

För att lyckas i rollen behöver du ta kliv i att utveckla verksamheten genom att analysera, studera och omvärldsspana. Du är driven och uppskattar mixen av strategiskt och operativt arbete. Du kvalitetssäkrar och skruvar på arbetssätt och metoder för att utveckla verksamheten ännu mer. Att samverka internt och externt är grundläggande för rollen.
  
  

Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!
  
  

**Anställningsform:** Tillsvidareanställning.
  
**Varaktighet:** Tillsvidare.
  
  

Kultur- och fritidsförvaltningens uppdrag är att skapa förutsättningar för goda livsvillkor för alla Eskilstunas invånare och besökare genom att erbjuda ett brett kulturutbud och möjligheter till en stimulerande fritid. Vi ansvarar för kommunens kulturskola, bibliotek, mötesplatser, arkiv, museer, fritidsgårdar, föreningsstöd samt arenor för kultur, idrott och friluftsliv.
  
  

**Förvaltningen har ett förvaltningskontor och tre enheter:** område arenor och friluftsliv, område Ung fritid och mötesplatser samt område kultur.
  
  

Välkommen till Eskilstuna kommun - en av Sveriges mest hållbara kommuner. Vi vill samla modiga chefer och modiga medarbetare. Tillsammans med kompetenta och engagerade kollegor och chefer är du med och utvecklar Eskilstuna genom goda hållbara möten i vilken roll du än har. Du är med och driver förändring och varje dag gör du skillnad för människor. Vi välkomnar medarbetare med olika bakgrund och jobbar hårt för att våra arbetsplatser ska vara tillgängliga för alla.
  
  

Om du har skyddad identitet ansöker du genom att skicka in din ansökan skriftligen märkt Rekryteringsenheten eller lämna den i vår reception på Alva Myrdals gata 3D.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer, Developer Advocacy | Sweden | Remote
description:
Grafana Labs, the company behind the open observability cloud, is founded on the principles of open source, open standards, open ecosystems, and open culture. Grafana Cloud, our fully managed observability platform, is flexible and built for scale. With Grafana Cloud's actually useful AI, organizations can see, understand, and act on all their disparate data to move at the speed of their ambitions. Today, more than 35 million users and 7,000+ customers – including Anthropic, Bloomberg, NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Salesforce – trust Grafana Labs to ensure reliability of their applications and systems, resolve incidents quickly, and optimize their telemetry to reduce noise and cost. We are a 100% remote company with 1,600+ team members across 40+ countries, and we’re backed by leading investors including Lightspeed Venture Partners, Sequoia Capital, GIC, Coatue, J.P. Morgan, CapitalG, and Lead Edge Capital. Learn more at grafana.com and follow us on LinkedIn and X.



We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do.



You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity.

### **Senior ML Engineer Recommender Systems, Developer Advocacy | Sweden | Remote**



This is a fully remote position and we're considering candidates in Sweden.


**The Opportunity:**



Grafana Labs is building an Interactive Learning system, an open source, in-product learning experience that helps users learn and succeed without leaving Grafana. A central part of that vision is a personalized recommendation system that helps each user discover the next guide, action, or product experience most likely to help them succeed.



Today, the Interactive Learning tool includes a rule-based recommendation engine that provides useful contextual recommendations. We are hiring an ML Engineer to lead its evolution into an increasingly personalized, continuously improving system driven by real-time product behavior, content metadata, customer context, and experimentation.



This is an applied product data science role. You will personally build, deploy, and operate recommendation models, design experiments, establish evaluation methodology, and define the scientific roadmap. You will partner closely with software engineers who own the production recommender codebase and with an existing Data Analyst who supports measurement, instrumentation, and analysis across Developer Advocacy.


**What You’ll Be Doing:**



The long-term vision is ambitious, but we do not expect it to arrive in one release. We are looking for someone who can understand the whole problem, establish strong foundations, and ship measurable improvements into the existing recommender one iteration at a time.


* **Evolve the Interactive Learning Plugin's recommendation system**
* + Develop increasingly personalized approaches to candidate selection, ranking, sequencing, and next-best-action recommendations.
	+ You’ll own a real-time recommendation service
* **Build and operate applied models**
* + Develop, validate, version, monitor, and iterate on models used by the recommendation system.
	+ You’ll own model training & serving
* **Define what recommendation quality means**
* + Develop offline, online, and longitudinal measures of recommendation performance.
	+ You’ll own feature pipelines, monitoring of the model and architecture
* **Ship incremental improvements**
* + Use the data and infrastructure available today while identifying the instrumentation and platform capabilities needed tomorrow.
	+ Integrate improvements into the existing recommender rather than waiting for a complete replacement system.
* **Partner across disciplines**
* + Work closely with software engineers & data analysts to productionize models and integrate them safely into the recommender service.
	+ Partner with the Product Analytics team on metric definitions, instrumentation, data quality, dashboards, and experiment analysis.
	+ Collaborate with Developer Advocacy, Docs, Product, Engineering, GTM, and other teams to translate ambiguous needs into testable hypotheses and measurable product decisions.
	+ Explain modeling choices, tradeoffs, uncertainty, and results clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.

**What Makes You a Great Fit:**



We know it is rare to find everything. Strong candidates should demonstrate credible ability across all three core areas below and be particularly strong in at least two.


* **Recommendation and personalization science**: you have built recommendation, ranking, search, matching, propensity, or next-best-action systems. You are comfortable beginning with simple, explainable approaches when they are the best way to learn.
* **HTTP/gRPC, streaming, Go/TypeScript previous experience in distributed systems**
* **Applied model ownership**. You have personally built, validated, monitored, and iterated on models used in a product or operational environment. You can work effectively in version-controlled codebases and collaborate with engineers on production implementation.


You should also be a strong product thinker and technical communicator. You can take an ambitious and ambiguous objective, identify the most important unknowns, and create a sequence of models and experiments that steadily improves the product.


**Bonus Points For:**


* Experience with content, education, onboarding, or learning recommendation systems
* Experience with SaaS product telemetry and customer-account data
* Experience using warehouse-scale behavioral data
* Experience with directed graphs, sequence models, or prerequisite-aware recommendations
* Experience with contextual bandits or other exploration strategies
* Familiarity with Grafana or the broader observability ecosystem
* Experience with open source software or transparent development practices
* Experience working with privacy, fairness, explainability, or responsible personalization constraints


**Compensation & Rewards:**


*In Sweden, the base compensation range for this role is SEK 775,444**- SEK 930,533**. Actual compensation may vary based on level, experience, and skillset as assessed throughout the interview process. All of our roles include Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), giving every team member ownership in Grafana Labs' success. We believe in shared outcomes—RSUs help us stay aligned and invested as we scale globally.*


* *Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process.*


**Why You’ll Thrive at Grafana Labs:**


* **100% Remote, Global Culture -** As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose.
* **Scaling Organization** – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment.
* **Transparent Communication** – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates.
* **Innovation-Driven** – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things.
* **Open Source Roots** – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work.
* **Empowered Teams** – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics.
* **Career Growth Pathways** – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career.
* **Approachable Leadership** – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human.
* **Passionate People** – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do.
* **In-Person onboarding** - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it.
* **Balance is Key** - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. *\*We will comply with local legislation where applicable.*


**Equal Opportunity Employer:** *Grafana Labs is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from everyone regardless of race, colour, nationality, origin, caste, sex, gender reassignment identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, religion or belief, disability, veteran status, genetic information, pregnancy, maternity, marital, family or carer status, or any other characteristic which is protected by local law. We believe that equality and diversity build a strong organisation, and we work hard to ensure that is the foundation of our organisation as we grow.*


*Grafana Labs may utilize AI tools in its recruitment process to assist in matching information provided in CVs to job postings. The recruitment team will continue to review inbound CVs manually to identify alignment with current openings.*



#LI-Remote



*For information about how your personal data is used once you’ve applied to a job, check out our* *privacy policy**.*

title: Senior Android Developer
description:
##### **Project description**

DXC Luxoft is a leading global software services company, delivering professional services across multiple business verticals such as finance, automotive, and digital transformation.  

  

The Automotive practice inside DXC Luxoft delivers SW projects to a majority of the world's car manufacturers and suppliers for both personal and commercial vehicles. We are known for our Automotive SW expertise and for helping our customers with high quality services and deliveries.  

Currently we are growing for our Swedish customers and are looking for Android Developers to join our team. You will be part of our growing organization in Sweden and will be part of developing next gen systems for the Automotive industry for the age of connected, autonomous, electric vehicles.  

  

The scope of work is the adaptation of the Android applications related to Charging and Car status for the Infotainment system to a new car platform with another screen size and orientation.


##### **Responsibilities**


As an Android Infotainment Software Developer, you will be responsible for implementing a complete feature for the infotainment system, spanning from software development in application and native layers of Android stack which includes hardware abstraction layer in C++. You will use the latest tools and technologies available and collaborate with UX, partners and other stakeholders to define and deliver coherent end-to-end solutions. You have end-to-end responsibility including design, architecture, documentation, implementation, verification, and validation. The verification and validation of the software involve automation tests that run in CI environment, as well as manual testing in rigs and cars. As part of R&D organization you will have the opportunity to actively provide innovative solutions for our products.


##### **Skills**


Must have


Extensive experience with Android Application development.


At least 4 years of software development in Java/Kotlin.


M.Sc in Software Engineering or similar relevant experience.


Experience with AOSP and the AOSP build system.


Familiarity with modern app architectural patterns (MVP, MVVM, clean architecture).


Experience from working in a Linux environment.


Experience in developing automated unit or integration test in Java/Kotlin.


Experience in working with Git/Gerrit/Jenkins tools.


Good English skill (written and spoken).


Experience within Automotive, Infotainment and from working in an international environment.


Experience working with UX/UI team for app development.


Nice to have


Knowledge in tools as; Jira, Car Weaver and Teamcenter.


Software development in application and native layers of Android stack which includes hardware abstraction layer in C++.


Driver's license (B-level).


##### **Other**


Languages


English: C1 Advanced


Seniority


Senior


  
Gothenburg, Sweden


Req. VR-124134


Android


Automotive Industry


22/07/2026


Req. VR-124134

title: Systemutvecklare till Jordbruksverkets integrationsplattform
description:
**Om arbetsplatsen**
--------------------


Jordbruksverket finns för att främja en ökad och hållbar matproduktion och god djurvälfärd i hela Sverige. Vi bidrar till en levande landsbygd. Hos oss arbetar cirka 1850 personer. Huvudkontoret ligger i Jönköping men vi har även stor regional verksamhet.
  

  

Vi arbetar aktivt för att våra medarbetare ska vara hållbara och trivas på jobbet! Vi investerar långsiktigt i våra medarbetare och vill att de utvecklas tillsammans med oss! Vi erbjuder en god arbetsmiljö med generösa förmåner med upp till 35 semesterdagar, friskvårdsbidrag samt friskvårdstid. I en stor del av våra anställningar erbjuder vi en flexibel arbetstid, möjlighet att jobba mobilt och arbeta delar av arbetstiden på distans.
  

  

Läs mer om hur det är att arbeta på Jordbruksverket på vår webbplats: www.jordbruksverket.se/jobbahososs


**Arbetsuppgifter**
-------------------


Jordbruksverket söker en till två drivna utvecklare till vårt ICCteam som ansvarar för myndighetens integrationer mot interna och externa system. I rollen blir du en central länk i samarbetet med olika parter, både internt och externt, och du bidrar med din kommunikativa förmåga för att säkerställa smidiga och pålitliga integrationer. Du kommer att arbeta i en agil miljö där du tillsammans med teamet planerar, utvecklar och förvaltar lösningar som stödjer vår verksamhet och våra användare.
   

  

Det dagliga arbetet innebär utveckling i Java och du kommer arbeta med tekniker som Camel Quarkus, ApacheArtemis, JSON, Angular, Nexus och WildFly. Det är inte ett krav att behärska samtliga tekniker, men en stark vilja att lära och anpassa dig till nya verktyg är viktig. Du deltar i sprintplaneringar, dagliga standupmöten och retrospektiv och bidrar aktivt till att förbättra teamets arbetsmetoder och kompetensutveckling.


**Kvalifikationer**
-------------------


Vi söker dig med en relevant akademisk ITutbildning eller har förvärvat motsvarande kompetens genom flerårig erfarenhet av liknande arbetsuppgifter. Det är meriterande om du har flera års erfarenhet som systemutvecklare samt om du har arbetat i en större organisation.
   

  

Som person är du analytisk, initiativtagande och kvalitetsmedveten, med ett starkt engagemang och ansvarskänsla. Du ser helheten och tänker långsiktigt samt är nyfiken på nya tekniker. Du arbetar tillsammans med teamet för att gemensamt coacha varandra, ge och ta emot feedback samt främja utveckling och lärande. Vi erbjuder en stimulerande arbetsmiljö på en myndighet med starkt samhällsuppdrag, där du får påverka viktiga digitala lösningar och arbeta i en kultur som värdesätter samarbete, kvalitet och en positiv inställning till teamarbete.
  

  

Goda kunskaper i svenska, både i tal och skrift, är ett grundläggande krav.
  

  

Vi intervjuar kandidater löpande.
   

  

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!


**Anställningens omfattning**
-----------------------------


Tillträde enligt överenskommelse


**Ansökan**
-----------


Referensnummer: 2.4.6-13065/2026

  



**Lön**
-------


Löneform: Månadslön.


**Anställning**
---------------


Arbetstid: Dagtid.


**Övrigt**
----------


Placeringsort är Jönköpingmed möjlighet att arbeta delar av arbetstiden på distans.
  

  

För oss är det viktigt att all kompetens på arbetsmarknaden tillvaratas. Vi välkomnar alla sökande och eftersträvar mångfald. Vi tillämpar normalt 6 månaders provanställning.
  

  

Vi tillämpar individuell lönesättning beroende på din kompetens, erfarenhet och ansvar i enlighet med de centrala löneavtalen RALS/RALS-T.
  

  

Inför rekryteringsarbetet har vi tagit ställning till rekryteringskanaler och marknadsföring. Vi vill därför inte bli kontaktade av annonsförsäljare eller rekryteringsföretag.


Vill du veta var vi finns?
  

Se arbetsplatsen på karta här.


  
**Generell information**
------------------------

**Arbetstid:** Dagtid.
  
  

Placeringsort är Jönköpingmed möjlighet att arbeta delar av arbetstiden på distans.
  
  

För oss är det viktigt att all kompetens på arbetsmarknaden tillvaratas. Vi välkomnar alla sökande och eftersträvar mångfald. Vi tillämpar normalt 6 månaders provanställning.
  
  

Vi tillämpar individuell lönesättning beroende på din kompetens, erfarenhet och ansvar i enlighet med de centrala löneavtalen RALS/RALS-T.
  
  

Inför rekryteringsarbetet har vi tagit ställning till rekryteringskanaler och marknadsföring. Vi vill därför inte bli kontaktade av annonsförsäljare eller rekryteringsföretag.

title: Creative Front-end Developer
description:
We're Omoi — a creative agency for deep tech. Space, aerospace, AI, robotics, energy — we help companies with branding, strategy, design, content, and storytelling. Companies doing hard things that are hard to explain. But also companies that, every day, achieve the near-impossible.
 




  






 Since we started Omoi two years ago, we've worked with over 25 companies across 7+ countries, grown from two people to twelve, and helped our clients raise more than €300 million in capital.
 




  






 We're looking for a
 
**Creative Front-end Developer** 

 . Someone who loves building beautiful, performant digital experiences and believes the best websites are crafted, not just coded. You're someone who bridges the gap between design and technology — turning thoughtful concepts into polished, interactive experiences. You notice when typography is slightly off, when spacing doesn't feel right, or when an interaction could be smoother. You're comfortable designing and refining interfaces, and making thoughtful visual decisions without needing every pixel handed to you.
 




  





 The work spans brand websites, product sites, and digital experiences, built primarily in Webflow, reaching for creative code whenever an idea demands it. You'll work closely with our designers to turn concepts into polished, responsive experiences while contributing your own design thinking throughout the process.
 



  





 We build our own things too — internal tools, experiments, and the occasional product we might take to market. Fast, functional prototypes are great, but someone eventually needs to make them feel intentional. That's where you come in. You'll help shape products that are not only well-built, but enjoyable to use.
 



  





 We're embracing AI as part of how we work. Tools like Claude Code are part of our everyday workflow, but knowing how to prompt isn't enough. We value people who know when AI gets you 80% of the way there—and have the craft to take it the final 20%.
 



  





 This role is not for the faint of heart. We're a startup working for startups. Founders trust us with the future of their businesses, and we take that responsibility seriously. It takes curiosity, ownership, resilience, and a constant drive to make things better than they were yesterday.
 



  





 We don't count years. Show us what you've built. We want to see websites that feel alive. Products that are fast, polished, and carefully considered. Interactions you obsessed over until they felt just right.
 



  





 Our working language is English, and all work happens from our studio in central Stockholm. We don't do remote work—we believe the best creative work comes from collaborating in person.
 



  





 You'll be joining a team that has worked around the world at companies including Bottega Veneta, Ogilvy & Mather, Gin Lane, Great Works, Kurppa Hosk, Laird & Partners, Collins, and more.
 



  





 To see examples of what we do, visit omoi.co. (It's not fully updated because… you're not here yet.)
 



  





 Please send your CV together with links to your portfolio, GitHub, and any live websites or products you've built. We care far more about what you've made than how long you've been making it. The role is available immediately.
 



  





**Note: All applications must link to a portfolio with links to work completed to be considered for this role.** 




  





 What we are looking for:
 


* Strong front-end development skills with experience building polished, production-ready websites and digital experiences
* Excellent Webflow skills, with the ability to independently build responsive, performant websites from concept through launch
* Solid HTML, CSS, and JavaScript fundamentals
* A strong eye for typography, layout, spacing, motion, and interaction design
* Comfortable designing websites, refining interfaces, and making thoughtful design decisions when needed
* Experience translating Figma designs into pixel-perfect, responsive builds while knowing when to improve on the details
* An instinct for craft—the difference between a site that works and one that feels alive
* Comfortable building with AI tools like Claude Code as a genuine part of your workflow, while knowing when manual craftsmanship still matters
* Bonus: experience with GSAP, Canvas, Three.js/WebGL, React, or other creative coding frameworks
* Bonus: experience working with deep-tech, science, or technology companies

title: Fullstack Developer
description:
With over 40 million visits each month, you’ll be part of Sweden’s most popular property platform – and one of the world’s most admired real estate platforms. With access to vast amounts of data, you’ll have limitless opportunities to explore and create new features that enhance the experience for Sweden’s real estate agents, home buyers, and sellers.
   

  

 You’ll join a collaborative, cross-functional team where your unique perspective is truly valued. As a senior engineer, you’ll lead by example by crafting elegant code while driving architectural evolution, mentoring colleagues, and helping us build a modern, scalable platform.
   

  

**What You’ll Do 💥**
 At Hemnet, we help millions of people navigate one of the biggest milestones of their lives: finding and shaping their home journey. Our team’s mission is to make that experience feel personal, inspiring, and seamless.
   

  

 We Are Looking For a Collaborative Backend Engineer To Help Us Build The Real-time Data Pipelines And Smart CRM Integrations That Deliver The Right Message To The Right User At Exactly The Right Time. You'll
   

  

* Build & Scale with Node.js: Design and evolve resilient backend services and APIs using Node.js, Nest.js, and GraphQL, keeping our platform fast and reliable.
* Connect Our Systems: Lead the technical direction of our CRM and marketing automation integrations. You’ll ensure our data pipelines are secure, scalable, and built to last.
* Bring Data to Life: Partner closely with our CRM and Marketing teams to turn user behavioral signals and segmentations into timely, meaningful automated messages.
* Collaborate Across Disciplines: Act as a supportive technical partner for non-technical stakeholders, translating complex backend concepts into clear, shared ideas.
* Grow Together: Lift the team around you through empathetic mentorship, open knowledge sharing, and contributing to a welcoming, continuous-learning engineering culture.


**Who we think you are** 
 🤩
   

  

**Role**
 We know that excellent engineers come from all kinds of backgrounds. You don’t need to match a rigid checklist, but we think you’ll thrive in this role if:
   

  

* You have a solid foundation in backend development, with deep experience in Node.js and framework ecoystems like Nest.js.
* You enjoy the unique challenges of third-party integrations, API design, and event-driven data flows.
* You care just as much about the people you work with as the code you write, you value clear communication, psychological safety and diverse perspectives.
* Bonus points (but not required): You’ve worked with Ruby, or you’re curious about experimenting with modern AI tooling like Claude.


**I am your new manager 👋**
 Hi!
   

  

 My name is Staffan, and I’ll be your new manager. I’ve been at Hemnet since 2019 and since last year working as Engineering Manager for a domain called Consumers. Our teams are currently working on many new and important areas for Hemnet – including personalization and smarter search features (including several initiatives utilizing AI to deliver next level user experience).
   

  

 My leadership style is listening, transparent, accessible, and supportive, while also challenging you to take your next step. Hopefully, we’ll learn a lot from each other and have plenty of fun as we break new ground at Hemnet. As a team, we get things done; we know when and where value can be added, and we’re clear on our priorities.
   

  

 Why did I decide to join Hemnet? I was strongly drawn to the culture and atmosphere – a wonderful combination of kindness, inclusiveness, and helpfulness, paired with high ambitions and a strong drive forward. My impression is that the people and the workplace together create the perfect foundation for you to do the best work of your career.
   

  

**A Note on Inclusion 💚**
 At Hemnet, we believe that diversity in gender, sexual orientation, ability, neurodiversity, cultural background and life experience makes us stronger. We welcome applicants of all identities and backgrounds.
   

  

 We build better together. At Hemnet, we believe diverse teams write better code and build better products. Even if you don’t check every single box above, we encourage you to apply. We value potential, curiosity, and a willingness to learn just as much as past experience.
   

  

 Our friendly Hemnet culture fosters a dynamic, playful environment where every voice is valued. Here, you’ll join a welcoming team that encourages you to thrive and grow, working alongside passionate colleagues from around the world.
   

  

**An Office-First Culture 🙏**
 We believe in the power of connection and the importance of flexibility. Our central Stockholm office is our hub for collaboration three days a week, with two remote days to support your personal work-life balance. We prioritize a sustainable pace because we know our best work happens when we’re energized and inspired.
   

  

*Please note that we're unable to provide relocation assistance for this opportunity.*
 Hemnet is Sweden’s largest property platform. Since the start in 1998, we have been the natural meeting place for real estate agents, property sellers and buyers. Hemnet offers the majority of properties for sale on the market; the largest number of potential buyers; products through which sellers are able to influence the selling of their property - and products that give real estate agents the opportunity to strengthen their brand. We are one of Sweden’s largest sites and apps, and one of the most popular property platforms in the world based on population.

title: Backend Developer
description:
At Hemnet, we build one of Sweden’s most widely used digital services. Every day, we help people navigate some of life’s biggest decisions, and that brings high expectations for quality, reliability, and long-term thinking.
   

  

 We’re now looking for a Fullstack Developer to join our B2B & Seller Domain. This team builds products and services that help homeowners and agents make the most of their opportunities in the housing market.
   

  

**About The Role 🍏**
 You’ll be part of an experienced, cross-functional team working across both user-facing features and backend systems. You’ll contribute through hands-on development, technical leadership, and close collaboration with Product Managers, Designers, Analysts, and Engineers. Together, you’ll build solutions that create real value for sellers.
   

  

 You’ll design, build, and maintain scalable backend services using Ruby, Node.js and Nest.js, contribute to frontend development in React, and support and evolve APIs across GraphQL and REST. You’ll also play an active role in our transition toward a more service-oriented architecture, helping break down complex problems and move monolithic systems into modular services over time.
   

  

 Beyond day-to-day development, you’ll take part in technical discussions, contribute to architectural direction, support other engineers through collaboration and knowledge sharing, and help us continuously improve code quality, performance, and developer experience. We also hope you’ll contribute to a team culture where different perspectives are valued and people can grow together.
   

  

**Who We Think You Are 🤩**
 Our current stack includes Ruby and Node.js on the backend and Next.js on the frontend. You’ll work with Ruby in this role, but we don’t expect you to know everything from the start.
   

  

 We think you’re an experienced engineer who enjoys building production-grade systems and working through complex problems together with others. You care about code quality, maintainability, and creating solutions that hold up over time.
   

  

 You’re comfortable contributing to technical decisions, and you like collaborating across disciplines to build products that are useful and thoughtful. You don’t need to tick every box on day one, what matters most is that you bring solid engineering experience, a learning mindset, and a generous way of working with others.
   

  

**What You’ll Do 💥**
* Build and improve Hemnet’s products across the system.
* Take part in technical discussions and architectural decisions
* Work closely with product, design, and analytics.
* Contribute to a sustainable pace, high quality, and a great developer experience.
* You’ll use AI tools as part of your day-to-day work, for example to explore ideas, speed up implementation, improve code quality, and support learning and collaboration across the team.


**I am your new manager 👋**
 Hi!
   

  

 My name is Tomas, and I’ll be your new manager. I’ve been at Hemnet since 2022 and am currently working as Engineering Manager in our domain called B2B & Sellers. I’m really excited to support the teams that will be working on many new and important areas for Hemnet, including building tools and products for agents to help them make more informed decisions.
   

  

 My leadership style is listening, transparent, accessible, and supportive, while also challenging you to take your next step. Hopefully, we’ll learn a lot from each other and have plenty of fun as we break new ground at Hemnet. As a team, we get things done; we know when and where value can be added, and we’re clear on our priorities.
   

  

 Why did I decide to join Hemnet? I was strongly drawn to the culture and atmosphere – a wonderful combination of kindness, inclusiveness, and helpfulness, paired with high ambitions and a strong drive forward. My impression is that the people and the workplace together create the perfect foundation for you to do the best work of your career.
   

  

**A Note on Inclusion 💚**
 At Hemnet, we believe that diversity in gender, sexual orientation, ability, neurodiversity, cultural background and life experience makes us stronger. We welcome applicants of all identities and backgrounds.
   

  

 If you meet many but not all of the requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value curiosity, a strong sense of purpose and the motivation to learn and grow, alongside your skills and experiences.
   

  

**A Hybrid Way of Working 🚲**
 Our vibrant office in central Stockholm is the hub of our collaboration and culture, providing a lively space that fosters team spirit. We come together in the office three days a week, with two flexible remote days. To be part of our team, it’s essential to be based in or near Stockholm.
   

  

*Please note that we're unable to provide relocation assistance for this opportunity.*
 Hemnet is Sweden’s largest property platform. Since the start in 1998, we have been the natural meeting place for real estate agents, property sellers and buyers. Hemnet offers the majority of properties for sale on the market; the largest number of potential buyers; products through which sellers are able to influence the selling of their property - and products that give real estate agents the opportunity to strengthen their brand. We are one of Sweden’s largest sites and apps, and one of the most popular property platforms in the world based on population.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Craft:**
 Technology & Development
   

  

**Job Description:**
 King's second largest game, Candy Crush Soda Saga, is loved by millions of players worldwide, matching Swedish fish, rescued Soda gummy bears, and popped soda bottles to win points, defeat obstacles and progress through almost 20,000 levels. We're an iconic team growing a sweeter future for one of the biggest casual mobile IPs in the world through attracting talented people from around the world. Our ambition is to keep delivering for our players when it comes to creativity, product, and technology.
   

  

 As a
 **C++ Developer** 
 working on Monetisation in Candy Crush Soda Saga's Gameplay and Monetisation Domain, you'll help build and evolve the systems that drive player growth and IAP performance for one of King's biggest live games. You'll design, build and test monetisation features that millions of players experience every day - from purchase flows to progression-linked offers and the systems that make spending feel relevant and worthwhile.
   

  

 Your Role Within the Kingdom
   

  

* Design, build, test, maintain and optimise monetisation features and systems in Soda's live mobile game - from purchase flows and offer systems to live-ops content that drives engagement and revenue.
* Deliver high-quality C++ code with strong focus on quality, reliability and performance.
* Work on complex engineering problems, applying sound judgment to choose practical solutions and ship them at a healthy cadence.
* Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Designers, UX/UI, Data Scientists and fellow engineers to shape monetisation experiences that genuinely add value for players as well as the business - and that are quick to test, learn from and iterate on.
* Use live data and A/B test results to validate your work and inform your next decisions - experimentation is at the heart of how this team operates.
* Share knowledge with teammates, support more junior colleagues, take part in code and design reviews, and engage with engineering forums across Soda.
* Use AI development tools as part of how you build, debug and review code - or be keen to adopt them.


 Soda is a large, mature C++ codebase delivered to millions of mobile players across Android and iOS. You'll work across the game client and the internal tools that power monetisation, alongside King's shared platforms for analytics, experimentation and live operations.
   

  

 Skills to Create Thrills
   

  

 Key Attributes
   

  

* Curious, analytical and motivated to understand how the systems you work on connect to the player experience and the business.
* Humble, flexible and collaborative - contributes openly in a cross-craft team, gives clear updates, and welcomes feedback.
* Passion for programming with a strong commitment to quality and polish in your code.


**Experience**
* Hands-on experience in software development, able to deliver standard engineering work to a high standard.
* Experience building, testing and maintaining software features in a production environment.
* Experience in game development, mobile development, or performance-critical applications is a plus - as is exposure to monetisation systems, live ops, or A/B test-driven work.


 Knowledge & Skills
   

  

* Strong C++ skills, with enjoyment of working closely with the language to build features and systems.
* Solid understanding of software engineering principles, with the ability to solve a wide range of technical issues in practical and creative ways.
* Sound coding fundamentals - writing high-quality, well-structured, maintainable code and applying effective testing practices.
* Comfort using AI development tools (e.g. Claude Code, Copilot) - or willingness to adopt them - as part of day-to-day engineering work.
* Comfort working in an experimentation-driven environment - able to make decisions informed by A/B test results and live data.


 How We Work
   

  

 Our Team First, Hybrid Working model empowers teams across the Kingdom to decide how they want to work together, with a mix of in office and home working.
   

  

 If you're excited about this role but don't meet every single requirement, we'd still love to hear from you. We know that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds may hesitate to apply unless they match every point, and we're interested in your potential as well as your experience.
   

  

**About King**
 With a mission of
 *Making the World Playful* 
 , King is a leading interactive entertainment company for the mobile world with more than 20 years of history of delivering some of the world’s most iconic games in the mobile gaming industry, including the world-famous Candy Crush franchise, as well as other mobile titles such as
 *Farm Heroes Saga* 
 . King games are played by more than 200 million monthly active users. King, part of Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT), has Kingsters in Stockholm, Malmö, London, Barcelona, Berlin, Dublin, San Francisco, New York, Los Angeles and Malta. More information can be found at King.com or by following us on LinkedIn and @lifeatking on Instagram.

title: Senior Backend Engineer
description:
Svea Solar is on the verge of transforming how we manage energy with just the click of a button. We're here to help our customers make the most of their energy usage while also balancing the energy grid with smart, innovative solutions. To bring this vision to life, we're expanding our engineering team and are on the lookout for our next Backend Engineer.
   

  

**About The Role & The Team**
 As a Backend Engineer, you'll join our Sunbeam Labs, where we’re creating digital services that range from energy optimization to virtual power plants. You’ll work closely with a talented and energetic team of engineers and domain experts, all focused on building cutting-edge software solutions that make energy optimization a reality for our customers.
   

  

 In this role, you’ll be involved in every stage of the software development lifecycle, from brainstorming ideas to deployment and ongoing maintenance. Since we’re pioneering services that haven’t existed before, there’s plenty of room for discovery and problem-solving! You’ll be working with thousands of connected devices that we control in real-time, based on measurements taken multiple times per second. Your daily challenges might include figuring out how to distribute work among devices, handling AWS datacenter failovers without a hitch, counting milliseconds and megawatts, and even predicting tomorrow’s sunshine.
   

  

 If you love tackling challenges, writing code, and having fun while doing it, this could be the
   

  

 perfect fit for you!
   

  

 🌍 Life as a Power Shifter - What do we offer you
   

  

**The opportunity to make an impact** 
 – your work directly contributes to fixing the planet and driving the energy transition.
   

  

**About**
**A value-driven workplace** 
 – read more about our values here.
   

  

**The chance to own your results** 
 – grow, take responsibility, and power up as both we and the energy industry evolve.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusivity** 
 – different perspectives drive innovation. Here, you can be yourself and contribute with your experiences, identities, and ideas.
   

  

**Employment benefits** 
 – occupational pension, 30 days of vacation, wellness allowance, parking, and more.
   

  

**Flexibility / hybrid work** 
 – work up to two days a week from home.
   

  

 Want to see what life at Svea Solar really looks like? Follow us on Instagram: @LifeAtSveaSolar
   

  

 Are we a match?
   

  

 At Svea Solar, we’re all about our values, and we want to make sure they resonate with you too. We’re looking for a Senior Backend Engineer who’s not just experienced, but who’s also passionate about end-to-end development. You should be comfortable with architecture styles and APIs (like REST or RPC) and have strong communication skills with a proactive attitude.
   

  

**Here’s what we’re looking for:**
* You value team success over personal success.
* 10+ years of experience as a backend developer, preferably with .NET and C#
* A solid understanding of architecture styles and APIs
* Great communication skills


**Bonus points if you have:**
* Knowledge of cloud technologies (we use AWS)
* Experience in the energy sector
* Experience with Kubernetes and Docker
* Experience with time series databases
* Experience working with physical devices


**We are a hybrid team that meets at our office in Alvik Strand 3 times a week.**
 If this sounds like you, we’d love to meet you! Join us on our mission to revolutionize home energy solutions and create a greener, more sustainable future.
   

  

 ⚡Who are we
   

  

 As Sweden’s - and one of Europe’s - leading providers of energy tech and solar, we help our customers optimize their energy use with smart, connected solutions. From solar panels to heat pumps, batteries, EV chargers, and software, we’re shaping the future of energy. We call it
 **The Power Shift** 
 . And the ones driving it?
 **Power Shifters** 
 .
   

  

  

  

**About Our Recruitment Process**
* With structured interviews and assessments through Alva Labs, we make sure our recruitment process is fair, inclusive, and unbiased - because diversity is key to innovation!
* Read more about our Recruitment process here.


 👉
 **Ready to become a Power Shifter?** 
 We review applications on a rolling basis, so don’t wait to send yours in!

title: Backend Developer in Digital Security
description:
**Are you passionate about digital security? We are looking for you!**
 In Swedbank you have the opportunity to:
   

  

* Analyse, design, develop, test and maintain services delivered by the team
* Ensure quality, accessibility and care when designing, building and maintaining software
* Work with architectural design in collaboration with architects
* Participate in a feedback culture where you both review and receive feedback to improve code quality
* Collaborate with the product owner, business analyst and other roles to refine and prioritise the product backlogs
* Handle maintenance and operations of our active services in accordance with existing processes
* Work in a value stream where Harmonize is our way of working


 What is needed in this role:
   

  

* 3-5 years of experience with Java and Spring Boot
* A good understanding of clean architecture, API design, microservices, and distributed systems
* A great team player with a collaborative mindset who works well in mob/pair programming
* Interest in adopting and experimenting with AI as a development tool
* Able to communicate efficiently in English
* A solution-oriented person with a customer-centric and business value driven mindset.
* Self- sufficient and self-organized with a desire to continuously improve.
* Capability to deliver in a changing and fast-moving environment.


 With us, you can experience:
   

  

* Personal and professional growth through self-leadership and continuous development.
* Meaningful work that positively impacts our workplace, our customers, and society.
* An open and collaborative culture that encourages cross-functional teamwork and provides networking opportunities.
* A supportive and inclusive environment that promotes a balanced and sustainable work-life, with flexible working conditions when suitable for the role.
* Benefits such as our share based reward program Eken, company pension plan, employee offer for banking products, health insurance.


 "Join our team and...
   

  

 become part of an inclusive and high‑performing team that constantly strives to improve how we work. We are responsible for Swedbank’s digital login methods, including BankID and fraud prevention in customer channels. Our mission is to ensure that every customer feels safe and confident when using Swedbank’s digital channels, a responsibility that truly drives us.
   

  

 Right now, we are focused on increasing efficiency, sharing our knowledge more broadly, and continuing to deliver at a consistently high level as a high‑performance team
 **” Tove Zander** 
 ,
 **your future manager**
 We look forward to receiving your application by 09.08.2026.
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm
   

  

**Recruiting manager:** 
 Tove Zander
   

  

 We may begin the selection during the application period, so we welcome your application as soon as possible.
   

  

 We would like to let you know that a background check and a drug test may be a part of the process for this role.
   

  

 We have made our choice regarding recruitment media and therefore kindly decline contact with ad sellers or sellers of other recruitment services.
   

  

 We want to inform you that the selection process may begin after the summer holidays.
   

  

**Swedbank does not discriminate anybody based on gender, age, sexual orientation or sexual identity, ethnicity, religion or disability – everybody is welcome.**

title: Backend Developer – AI Platform & Editorial Tools
description:
Om Omni
   

  

 Omni har funnits på den svenska mediemarknaden sedan november 2013 och är ett självständigt bolag inom Schibsted. Vi är Sveriges femte största nyhetstjänst med runt 800 000 unika läsare och har flera gånger utsetts till Sveriges bästa nyhetstjänst.
   

  

 Mediebranschen genomgår just nu en historisk förändring. AI förändrar hur journalistik produceras, organiseras och konsumeras. På Omni bygger vi nästa generations AI-drivna plattform och verktyg för nyhetsredaktioner – teknik som ska ge journalister helt nya möjligheter och lägga grunden för framtidens nyhetsupplevelser.
   

  

 Nu söker vi en backendutvecklare som vill vara med och bygga den tekniska plattformen bakom den utvecklingen.
   

  

 Rollen
   

  

 Du blir en del av ett litet, tvärfunktionellt team som bygger helt nya AI-drivna produkter och redaktionella verktyg. Här handlar arbetet inte om att förvalta befintliga system, utan om att utforska vad som blir möjligt när AI blir en självklar del av både utveckling och journalistik.
   

  

 Du bygger arkitektur, API:er, integrationer och intelligenta arbetsflöden som omsätter nya AI-möjligheter till robusta produkter. Du arbetar nära produkt och design, äger din kod hela vägen till drift och trivs i ett högt tempo där idéer snabbt blir verklighet.
   

  

 Här får du chansen att kliva in i ett sammansvetsat, prestigelöst team med stort eget ansvar och ett öppet klimat för nya idéer. Eftersom vi bygger nytt snarare än förvaltar befintliga system behöver du trivas med hög autonomi, snabba omställningar och en vardag där alla svar inte finns från början.
   

  

*Det här är en projektanställning på 12 månader med goda möjligheter till förlängning.*
 Vad du gör
   

  

* Utvecklar och arkitekterar API:er, backendtjänster och integrationer för våra AI-drivna produkter.
* Bygger och förbättrar event-drivna flöden för databehandling, automatisering och intelligenta arbetsflöden.
* Arbetar med LLM-baserade pipelines och hjälper till att göra dem tillförlitliga i produktion.
* Äger din kod hela vägen – från utveckling och test till deployment och drift.
* Är med och bygger den tekniska plattform som ska möjliggöra framtidens verktyg för nyhetsredaktioner.


 Vår stack
   

  

 Du Behöver Inte Vara Bekant Med Allt Från Dag Ett, Men Det Här Är Vad Du Jobbar Med
   

  

* Backend: Node.js med TypeScript, Hono, tRPC, Zod, Drizzle ORM
* Data: PostgreSQL, Neo4j, Redis
* AI: LLM-klienter och evals för kvalitetsmätning
* Event/orkestrering: AWS SQS, event-drivna workflows och queue handlers
* Frontend (vid behov): React, React Router, TanStack Query
* Infrastruktur: AWS, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, Docker och GitOps (ArgoCD)


 Utvecklingsverktyg som Claude Code och Codex är en hörnsten i vårt dagliga arbete och gör det möjligt för oss att bygga snabbare och smartare. Du behöver inte vara expert, men du har praktisk erfarenhet av AI-verktyg och en tydlig drivkraft att utforska hur de förändrar produktutveckling.
   

  

 Vi tror att du
   

  

* Har gedigen erfarenhet av backendutveckling, systemarkitektur och API-design.
* Är van att bygga och driftsätta produktionskritiska system.
* Har ett helhetsperspektiv på systemutveckling och gillar att lösa komplexa tekniska problem.
* Har erfarenhet av AI, LLM:er och moderna utvecklingsverktyg.
* Är en prestigelös lagspelare som uppskattar code reviews och pragmatiska lösningar.
* Trivs i ett högt tempo där nya idéer snabbt blir produkter.
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska.


 Det är ett plus om du även är bekväm i frontend, men tyngdpunkten ligger på backend.
   

  

 Hur vi jobbar
   

  

 Du blir en del av ett litet, sammansvetsat och snabbfotat team där vi värdesätter nyfikenhet, samarbete och ett högt tekniskt kunnande. Vi tror på pragmatiska lösningar, rak kommunikation och stort eget ansvar. Code reviews och kunskapsdelning är en självklar del av vårt arbetssätt.
   

  

 Omni tillämpar en hybrid arbetsmodell med utgångspunkt från vårt Stockholmskontor. Samtidigt har vi kollegor i bland annat Oslo, Gdansk och Marbella, och vi värnar om en flexibel arbetsvardag med god balans mellan arbete och fritid.

title: Lead SAP Software Engineer
description:
**Job Description**

**WHAT YOU’LL DO**




 As a
 **Lead SAP Software Engineer** 
 , you will lead the engineering direction of our master data team, building and modernizing the master data solutions which are the backbone of H&M's SAP landscape.
   

  





 You will provide technical leadership, define scalable solutions and architectures, mentor engineers across Stockholm and Bengaluru, and drive engineering excellence through modern development practices in
 **SAP ABAP, CPI, BTP** 
 etc. You will be working closely with Product Managers, Enterprise Architects, and business stakeholders, you will deliver secure, resilient, and high-performing master data solutions that enable critical business operations.
   

  





**WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH** 
  

  





 You will collaborate with Software Engineers, Product Managers, Enterprise Architects, Scrum Masters, and business stakeholders across global teams in Stockholm and Bengaluru. Together, you'll define technical roadmap of SAP master data solutions.
   

  





**WHO YOU ARE**




 We are looking for people with…
   

  




* 7+ years of software engineering experience, including experience in a Technical Lead or Staff Engineer role.
* Strong experience in
 **SAP ABAP Development** 
 , Object-Oriented ABAP, design patterns, clean coding principles, reusable frameworks, and enterprise application architecture.
* Strong experience in
 **SAP BTP Architecture** 
 , including the ability to architect composable, event-driven, and API-based solutions across SAP BTP and on-premises systems.
* Strong experience in
 **Clean Core implementation** 
 , SAP Application Extension Methodology, ABAP Cloud, In-App Extensibility, and Side-by-Side Extensions.
* Strong experience in
 **ABAP CDS** 
 , Code Pushdown, HANA Modelling, and performance optimization for effective data modelling and database management.
* Strong expertise in SAP BTP RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model), OData services, API development, and modern ABAP development practices.
* Strong hands-on experience with classical ABAP concepts including Reports, ALV, Data Dictionary, Function Modules, BAPIs, RFCs, IDocs, Enhancements, User Exits, Customer Exits, BAdIs, and interface development.
* Strong troubleshooting and ABAP debugging skills, including experience with complex production support scenarios, update debugging, background debugging, RFC debugging, SQL Trace (ST05), Runtime Analysis (SAT), performance tuning, and root-cause analysis.
* Proficiency in
 **unit testing with ABAP Unit** 
 , conducting code reviews, enforcing development standards, ensuring maintainable and high-quality code, and driving continuous improvement initiatives.
* Ability to mentor development teams, review technical designs, provide architecture guidance, and drive adoption of SAP best practices across projects and programs.



 And people who are responsible for…
   

  




* To provide simple, efficient and scalable
 **E-2-E SAP solutions** 
 primarily in SAP ECC, SAP MDG, SAP CRM systems which enables us to ensure reliable data of highest quality to all consuming applications.​
* To provide quick and efficient
 **Master Data Maintenance services** 
 and Roll Out of Master Data Solutions in Production systems primarily in SAP ECC, SAP MDG, SAP CRM systems.
* To design & develop New Information-Steward (IS) Reports and generate periodic IS reports for teams having Data monitoring requirements.



 Added Advantage to know..
   

  




* Hands-on experience with SAP Integration Suite, CPI, API Management, Event Mesh, Solace, Kafka, and event-driven integration architectures.
* Knowledge of SAP Fiori, SAPUI5, Fiori Elements, Gateway, and modern SAP UX technologies.



**WHO WE ARE**




 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future.
   

  





**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE**




 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer attractive benefits, flexible ways of working, and extensive opportunities for professional growth across our global organization.
   

  





 You'll work on business-critical enterprise platforms, collaborate with talented global engineering teams, and help shape the future of supply chain technology using modern cloud, integration, and AI-driven engineering practices.
   

  





**JOIN US**




 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.
   

  





 Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.

title: Senior Fullstack Developer (.NET/React)
description:
**Do you like to deliver technically advanced systems with complex business logic?** 
**We are now looking for a senior full-stack developer who wants to work with If's systems for our largest customers.**
**About The Role**
 As a full-stack developer, you work with C#, Entity Framework, React, and Azure to deliver a modern backend with .NET Core along with a high-performance frontend based on TypeScript and React. You have a strong focus on code quality, use AI tools in development effectively, and strive to deliver according to modern architecture patterns, as the team does today.
   

  

 You'll use your analytical and technical skills to solve advanced problems while contributing to our journey to the web and learning a lot of new things. Learning is important at If, which is why we offer a wide range of opportunities for learning such as tech seminars, skills days, digital learning and lots of internal training as well as opportunities for certifications.
   

  

 Within IT Industrial Core Systems, you are involved in developing systems that help our colleagues in If Industrial to manage our largest customers and their insurance for e.g. factories, department stores, freight and personnel.
   

  

**We offer**
* An including work environment where everyone is welcome
* Career and development opportunities in the biggest insurance company in the Nordics
* Social activities, as well as highly skilled professional environment
* Possibility of hybrid workplace
* Health promoting workplace with e.g., wellness allowance and various sports activities
* Great insurance benefits
* Lunch benefit
* High pension savings


**About The Team**
 You are a member of one of IT Industrial Core Systems' three agile development teams. Each team consists of four to five developers and a QA engineer who work together as a cross-functional agile team. The teams work closely with both product owners and architects to ensure that we deliver valuable features in the right way.
   

  

**Who are you?**
 We are looking for someone who is passionate about software development and wants to deliver high-quality code. You are self-motivated and are used to working with feedback to deliver the best possible results. You are good at communicating and collaborating to achieve common goals. You like to work with technically advanced systems and solve complex business logic in a smart way.
   

  

**You also have:**
* University education in computer science, systems development, IT or equivalent.
* Extensive knowledge of C#, .NET Core / Entity Framework / SQL Server.
* Extensive knowledge of the Azure platform.
* Experience in web development in TypeScript and React.
* Knowledge of agile development methods such as Scrum.
* Fluency in English.


**It is an advantage if you have (or want to learn):**
* Use of AI tools in development, such as GitHub Copilot.
* Domain-driven design (DDD).
* Proficiency in Swedish.


**More information and the recruitment process**
**Application** 
 : Submit your application with CV and answer the application questions no later than 2026-08-09. Screening and interviews will take place after the application deadline in August.
   

  

**Type of employment:** 
 Full-time permanent employment (100%).
   

  

**Location:** 
 Bergshamra, Stockholm.
   

  

**Start:** 
 By agreement.
   

  

 For more information, please contact Robert Gavelin, robert.gavelin@if.se

title: Business Developer
description:
Description: This role sits within the newly established Business Development and Strategy
 





 function in BID (Brand Identity Development), a key part of R&D responsible
 





 for developing brand-specific truck products and customer experiences. The position
 





 focuses on driving strategic business development and financial governance to support the
 





 transformation and growth of the BID organization. Working closely with CTO,
 





 BID management, and stakeholders across and TRATON R&D, the consultant will
 





 contribute to developing functional strategies, improving cross-functional collaboration, and
 





 supporting strategic decision-making. Key responsibilities include managing financial
 





 planning processes such as budgeting and forecasting, leading strategic finance initiatives,
 





 and ensuring effective collaboration between R&D, finance, and business functions. The role
 





 also involves supporting the TRATON Strategy PMO, managing the R&D management
 





 system, and driving prioritization and follow-up of strategic initiatives together with
 





 commercial stakeholders. Strong analytical capabilities, combined with data and AI-related
 





 skills, are important to successfully support business performance and strategic
 



* development

title: MuleSoft Integration Developer
description:
**About The Position**
 We are currently seeking an Integration Developer with strong expertise in MuleSoft Anypoint. Ideally, you also bring experience in platform and infrastructure management within AWS to support our growing Integration Solutions team. If you're passionate about building scalable integration systems, automating infrastructure, and working in a collaborative, open-minded team, this could be your next exciting challenge.
   

  

 In this role, your main responsibility will be integration development within MuleSoft Anypoint platform. You will be a key member of the development team, as well as collaborate closely with the infrastructure team to manage the platform infrastructure, ensuring seamless system integration and robust infrastructure management.
   

  

**Why this role matters**
 The Integration Solutions team is playing a key role in building a future-ready integration landscape. We have established a new Integration Competency Center (ICC) where you’ll be part of shaping the tools, processes, and infrastructure that support essential business operations across HR, Finance, Payroll, and more.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Design, develop, test, and deploy scalable and secure integration solutions using MuleSoft.
* Monitor and maintain platform performance with proactive incident and environment management.
* Collaborate with both IT and business stakeholders to translate needs into practical, scalable solutions.
* Work closely with Solution Architects and Integration Architects to continuously evolve our integration platform.
* Actively contribute to a transparent and agile team environment.


**Who You Are**
 We’re looking for someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment and values honest collaboration. You might be a great match if you:
   

  

* Are passionate about technology and creating well-functioning, scalable integration solutions.
* Drive integrations from concept to production with a focus on automation, scalability, and continuous improvement.
* A strong communicator who can explain complex technical concepts in simple terms.
* Are pragmatic, proactive, and always looking for opportunities to improve tools and processes.
* Enjoy working closely with others and helping build a collaborative team culture.
* Embrace feedback and continuous learning as part of your daily work.


**What You Bring**
 We're looking for a strong mix of technical skills and collaborative mindset. Ideally, you have:
   

  

* Hands-on experience designing and implementing integration solutions using tools like MuleSoft, APIs, and event-driven messaging.
* Familiarity with CI/CD tools (like GitHub Actions) and infrastructure-as-code (e.g., Terraform) is helpful, especially when collaborating with the infra team, but not essential.
* Strong problem-solving skills and a passion for creating maintainable, high-quality solutions.
* The ability to work independently while contributing actively to an agile, team-driven environment.


**Type of employment:** 
 Full-time, Permanent
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm / Oslo (hybrid), Krakow (remote)
   

  

*Due to the vacation period, we will begin reviewing applications in August. Applications will be evaluated on an ongoing basis from that time, and the position may be filled before the application deadline.*
 Schibsted is the largest news provider in the Nordics, with brands such as VG, Aftonbladet, TV4, MTV, Aftenposten, Svenska Dagbladet, and Podme. Every day we inform, engage, and entertain millions of people across Sweden, Norway, and Finland. Schibsted is covered by a collective bargaining agreement.
   

  

 Since 1839, trust has been our foundation. Our journalism empowers people, builds communities, and strengthens democracy – and innovation ensures we continue to protect that mission.
   

  

 Our democracies depend on independent journalism. That’s our business.

title: Founding Product Engineer
description:
**COMPANY DESCRIPTION** 





 NMBRD (The Numbered Closets) pairs a next generation AI assistant "Kora" with a network of shared walk-in closets.
 




  






 AI steps off the screen and into the physical world. Curated by Kora and the relationship she builds with our members. The Numbered Closets are community driven concept spaces where the coffee taste good and where the clothes fit its members lives and aspirations.
 




  






 Members subscribe to explore newness: they borrow pieces, drink specialty coffee, attend weekly events, and every interaction deepens the intelligence that drive our operations and our value to our partner brands.
 




  





 These shared closets are physical spaces designed for community and discovery, offering an evolving wardrobe shaped by real demand rather than long-term forecasting. By capturing real-time signals from members, NMBRD’s operating system generates highly accurate demand data, helping reduce waste in the fashion industry. The result is a smarter, more sustainable wardrobe experience for members and more meaningful insights for brands.
 



  





**Role Description** 




  





 The Founding Product Engineer is a full-time hybrid role based in Stockholm. This role involves designing, building, and iterating on core product features that power NMBRD’s assistant, shared closet experience, and underlying operating system. The Founding Product Engineer collaborates closely with design, operations, and community teams to translate member insights into usable, scalable product solutions, owning projects from concept through deployment. Daily tasks include hands-on product engineering, technical problem-solving, working on prototypes, and refining the user experience across digital and physical touchpoints. As a founding team member, this role also helps shape product strategy, engineering culture, and long-term technology decisions.
 



  





**Qualifications** 




  




* Strong Product Engineering skills, including experience building and shipping user-facing features and scalable systems.
* Analytical Skills and R&D capabilities to interpret data, experiment with new solutions, and validate ideas quickly.
* Product Design and communication skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams and translate member needs into intuitive experiences.
* Ability to conduct structured research into user behaviour, fashion trends, and operational workflows.
* Experience in early-stage startups or founding roles, with comfort in ambiguity and end-to-end ownership of projects.
* Proficiency in modern software development tools and practices (e.g., cloud services, version control, testing, and CI/CD).
* Interest in fashion, sustainability, or consumer technology, and a motivation to improve how people interact with their wardrobes.
* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.



  





**What this is not** 




  





 Not an employee req. This is founding-team work, with founding-team equity. We are pre-launch, small, and moving fast. You will build things that are live within 24 hours, wrong by next week, and better by the following week.
 



  





**Who do you work with** 




 You'll work in close partnership with our full-stack engineer and with NMBRD's founder.
 



  





**How to apply** 




 Meet Kora https://thenumberedclosets.com/ send us your cv, no cover letter please, and let us know how you would take her to the next phase. Make sure to use the same email on our site and when you send your application (We cross check that) >>>>
 ***join@numberedclosets.com***




  





 And btw we read everything, if you're as excited/serious about this as we are, you'll get feedback from us.

title: SVT söker ML/AI Engineer – Applied AI
description:
**Vad Vi Erbjuder Dig**
 SVT bygger nu nya team inom AI och automation för att frigöra tid, förenkla arbetssätt och ge publiken ännu bättre program och journalistik. Hos oss får du vara med i en långsiktig satsning där teknik används för att stärka public service, stötta journalistiken och göra verklig skillnad i en snabbt föränderlig omvärld.
   

  

 SVT:s AI-hubb stödjer hela organisationen i att använda AI på ett ansvarsfullt och värdeskapande sätt. Som ML/AI Engineer med fokus på Applied AI arbetar du nära redaktioner och produktionsteam. Du bidrar till generativa AI‑lösningar kopplade till journalistiska och innehållsnära behov, till exempel transkribering, metadata, sökförbättring, sammanfattningar och redaktionella verktyg. Rollen ger möjlighet att bygga lösningar som används i praktiken, med koppling till kvalitet, transparens och tillit.
   

  

 Rollen har fokus på generativa AI‑lösningar nära redaktion och innehållsproduktion.
   

  

**Vem Vi Tror Att Du Är**
 Du är analytisk och strukturerad med ett starkt fokus på kvalitet i det du bygger. Du är lösningsorienterad och driver arbetet från idé till stabil leverans i produktion.
   

  

 Du samarbetar väl med olika kompetenser, kommunicerar tydligt och är nyfiken på att utveckla AI‑lösningar som fungerar i praktiken.
   

  

**Du Behöver Ha**
* Erfarenhet av Python i produktionsmiljö och avancerade språk som tex Rust
* Erfarenhet av att bygga och driftsätta AI‑funktioner eller AI‑system som används i verksamhet eller av slutanvändare
* Erfarenhet av LLM‑baserade lösningar, till exempel prompt‑baserade arbetssätt, RAG eller agentmönster
* Erfarenhet av embeddings och semantisk sök/retrieval
* Erfarenhet av utvärdering och kvalitetssäkring av generativa system
* Erfarenhet av att ta lösningar från prototyp till stabil användning över tid
* Erfarenhet av samarbete med flera kompetenser, till exempel produkt, redaktion, juridik eller säkerhet
* Svenska eller engelska kan användas i arbetet. Svenska är meriterande och underlättar samarbetet med redaktioner.


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet av svenska språket i LLM‑ eller NLP‑sammanhang
* Erfarenhet av fine‑tuning eller anpassning av öppna modeller
* Erfarenhet av agentramverk eller verktygsintegrerade AI‑arbetsflöden
* Erfarenhet från medie‑ eller annan textintensiv verksamhet


**Vad Tjänsten Innebär**
 Du utvecklar generativa AI‑lösningar från idé till produktion tillsammans med Senior Engineer och Tech Lead. Arbetet omfattar prompts, retrieval‑flöden, agentbeteenden och integrationer mot interna verktyg och plattformar. Du bygger och underhåller utvärderingsupplägg som följer kvalitet över tid. Arbetet sker nära verksamheten och kan periodvis innebära arbete i redaktionell miljö, särskilt inom Nyheter & Sport. Rollen är på mid‑nivå och har inget arkitektur‑ eller personalansvar.
   

  

**Hur Du Ansöker**
 I den här rekryteringsprocessen använder vi urvalsfrågor och CV, du behöver alltså inte skicka med ett personligt brev. Det gör vi för att säkerställa att rekryteringen blir kompetensbaserad och effektiv. Frågorna handlar om dina erfarenheter och drivkrafter och kan ta en stund att besvara, men de gör att vi får samma viktiga information av alla kandidater. Välkommen med din ansökan senast 2026-06-24. Intervjuer sker löpande.
   

  

 För mer information om tjänsten kontakta Peter Kurzwelly, Head of AI, peter.kurzwelly@svt.se. Facklig information lämnas av Fanny Lindhé (SACO), Maria Nassikas (Unionen) och Jane Andersson (SJF). Samtliga nås via växeln, tfn 08-784 00 00.
   

  

**Annat Som Är Bra Att Veta**
 Tjänsten är placerad i Stockholm och i dagsläget har vi upplägget med minst 51 % närvaro på plats. Vi erbjuder en tillsvidareanställning på heltid.
   

  

**Välkommen med din ansökan**

title: AI Product engineer
description:
**About Napper** 





 Napper is a fast-growing startup building AI-powered experiences to help make bedtime easier for parents and children. The Napper app has been used by more than five million parents around the world and has more than 45,000 five-star reviews. The app is frequently featured on the App Store as an Editors’ Choice app, and has been App of the Day around the world.
 





 Our vision is ambitious; crafting and launching AAA-products that serve as essential digital partners for parents in their everyday life. Leveraging the latest technology combined with emphatic, human-centered design, we are scaling to become a household name for all families, everywhere. Our mission is to make sure each parent goes to bed at night feeling like the best parent in the world.
 





 We are backed by some of Sweden’s most prominent angel investors and two international top-tier VC firms. That being said, we are also cash flow positive.
 




  





**How we work** 





 We are a tight group of talented (arguably), fun (subjectively) and creative (definitively) people pushing the boundaries of product and business development. We are ambitious, but more importantly curious.  We ask questions. We try things. We ideate and iterate. And sometimes, we even argue. Because we
 
*feel* 

 for the things we build. 
 





 But most of all, we support each other. We laugh a lot. And we strive to make sure every person is really looking forward to coming into the office, every day. However, we also value freedom highly, and will cater to your needs whenever you need work to be flexible.
 




  





**What we look for** 





 You are a driven engineer who is excited to be part of our journey scaling Napper. You learn fast and are not afraid to take on new technical challenges. You actively seek responsibility and get motivated by complex problems and big tasks. You are looking for a job where you can advance your career fast and get well compensated for the hard work you put in. You love working with LLMs, data and machine learning, and find joy in creating outstanding user experiences.
 





 Our philosophy is to hire selectively and invest heavily in the people who work with us. If you become one of us - we’ll go above and beyond to make sure you find your work meaningful, inspiring, and stimulating, and feel fully supported by both your peers and superiors. We will evaluate your performance and re-negotiate your salary twice a year.
 




  





**What you will be doing** 





 You will work hand in hand with the CTO and CPO to explore what's truly possible with large language models and turn it into experiences millions of parents rely on at bedtime. As an AI Product Engineer at Napper, you'll be our creative force for AI: dreaming up bold ideas, rapidly prototyping them, and pushing models to their limits. You'll own the intelligence behind our product, shaping the AI and machine learning that power features like our sleep prediction engine, built on hundreds of millions of baby sleep logs. You'll spot opportunities others miss and turn them into features people love. We don't believe in micromanagement, so you'll have the freedom and the responsibility to follow your curiosity and make a real impact.
 




  






 Requirements
 



* 4+ years of experience working with software engineering or data science
* Demonstrable experience building with LLMs (prompts, evals, RAG, agents, or AI SDKs/APIs)
* Proficiency in Python and widely used machine learning and data engineering libraries (PyTorch, PySpark, Scikit-learn etc.)
* Team player who can work independently and proactively propose solutions when faced with ambiguous software engineering problems
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills



  





*Nice to have* 



* Experience with AWS and infrastructure as code



  







 ﻿
 
 What are you waiting for? We’d love to hear from you!

title: Product Designer
description:
**YOUR MISSION AT VOI**


Voi is making it easier to get around cities without a car. We build shared e-scooters, e-bikes and the software that powers them — from the rider app to the tools our field teams and warehouses use every day.


As a Product Designer, you will join a cross-functional team and own the experience of our products from discovery through to delivery. You will lead projects end-to-end, bringing a clear point of view, collaborating closely with your Product Manager and engineering team, and helping shape products that work for riders and cities alike.


You will take an insight-led approach, connect design decisions to business outcomes and proactively turn opportunities into meaningful improvements.

* Lead discovery and design for your team’s projects, from problem framing to shipped product.
* Frame hypotheses, explore a range of solutions and converge on clear, well-reasoned decisions.
* Plan and conduct user research, using qualitative and quantitative data to inform design decisions and evaluate impact.
