* Advocate for a high-quality, accessible and consistent user experience across Voi’s products.
* Contribute to roadmap planning, strategic discussions, ideation, user testing, implementation and feedback loops.
* Partner closely with Product, Engineering and other stakeholders to bring solutions to life.
* Contribute to design team rituals, processes and ways of working that strengthen design maturity across Voi.
* Explore new methods, analytics, metrics and AI tools, and apply what you learn in practice.

**WHAT YOU NEED TO EMBARK**


You are a thoughtful and proactive designer who enjoys working in fast-moving, cross-functional teams. You balance curiosity with clear decision-making, communicate your reasoning confidently and care about creating useful experiences grounded in real user needs.

* At least three years’ experience in product or UX design within cross-functional teams.
* Experience leading the design process from discovery and problem definition through to delivery and iteration.
* Ability to independently plan and run user research, and translate insights into design decisions.
* Experience using qualitative insights, analytics and product metrics to assess and improve your work.
* Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to explain design decisions to a range of stakeholders.
* A collaborative working style, alongside the confidence to take ownership and work independently.
* Comfort using AI tools as part of your everyday design and research process.
* Professional proficiency in English.


It would be great if you also have experience working with hardware or firmware, building and maintaining design systems, or conducting exploratory research beyond usability testing and validation.


Don’t meet every requirement? If you are excited about this role and our mission, and have relevant, transferable skills, we encourage you to apply – even if you don’t check every box.

**WHY VOI?**


Working at Voi is more than just a job; our People Promise includes a personal voyage where you will grow as a professional and be a part of a team and culture that builds something meaningful for society. In addition to this you’ll have the opportunity to:

* Join Europe’s #1 micromobility operator and one of the fastest growing scale-ups.
* Work with a supportive Product Design team of seven colleagues across diverse nationalities.
* Help shape the direction of sustainable urban transport and products used across Europe.
* Get “skin in the game” through our employee options programme.
* Enjoy unlimited free Voi rides and a dog-friendly office.
* Work from our central Stockholm office on Sveavägen, with flexible working hours and an expectation of three office days per week.
* Receive 30 days of paid holiday, a SEK 3,000 annual wellness allowance and the equipment you need to do your best work.
* Work together with inspiring, motivated and fun colleagues towards a common goal: creating sustainable cities made for living, free from noise and pollution.

title: Head of Design
description:
**About the role**


As Head of Design at 4C Strategies, you will lead a small, distributed team of designers and strengthen our design capability across product experiences, user workflows, and cross-functional collaboration. This is a hands-on leadership role: you are a skilled designer who both leads the team and keeps doing design work yourself, helping the team do their best work. Based in our Stockholm office, you will play a central role in shaping how design contributes to product strategy, customer value, and delivery across our Exonaut platform — our software for readiness and resilience — and related services. This role focuses on our Defence products, designing for armed forces and defence planners.


You will be responsible for building structure, quality, and consistency across the design function — balancing strategic direction with hands-on leadership and operational execution. The role involves close collaboration with Product Management, Engineering, Delivery Consultancy, and customer-facing teams to ensure user-centred, intuitive, and reliable solutions that support mission-critical environments.


As Head of Design, you will support and develop designers, strengthen design processes and governance, and ensure alignment between business priorities, customer needs, and product usability. You will help establish design as a key capability within 4C’s continued growth journey.

**The role at a glance**

* Location: Stockholm, Sweden (hybrid working).
* Travel: mainly regular travel to our Malmö office, plus 2–3 trips abroad per year (for example to our London, Warminster, or Orlando offices).
* Reports to: Chief Technology & Product Officer (CTPO).
* Team: a team of 3 designers, based in Malmö and Orlando (USA).
* Focus: Defence products, designing for armed forces and defence planners.
* Employment type: full-time, permanent (6-month probation).

**Key responsibilities**

* Work hands-on as a designer on key Defence product areas, contributing your own craft and raising quality together with the team.
* Lead and develop the team through regular 1:1s, coaching, feedback, and clear development plans, creating growth opportunities for each designer.
* Champion design quality, accessibility, and consistency across products, workflows, and customer-facing experiences.
* Improve design processes, design-system standards, and ways of working across cross-functional teams.
* Partner closely with Product Managers and Engineering to align user experience, technical feasibility, and business priorities.
* Turn customer and user insights — from continuous discovery and research — into intuitive, effective products, and measure success through clear outcomes and shared goals.
* Contribute to product strategy, keeping user-centred thinking at the heart of decisions, and set clear design principles for AI-enabled features (transparency, user control, and safe fallback options).
* Recruit and onboard designers, and support their growth through our design career framework, building an inclusive and collaborative team culture.

**About you**


You are an experienced design leader who combines deep user-centred thinking with structure, collaboration, and business understanding. You are comfortable operating in complex product environments where balancing user needs, technical realities, and organisational priorities is essential.


You enjoy building clarity and consistency across teams and processes while supporting designers in their growth and development. You are pragmatic, communicative, and motivated by improving both product experiences and ways of working. You lead by example — both doing design work and growing your team — and you measure your success by your team's quality, cohesion, and growth.

**We believe you bring**

* Experience leading or coaching designers within SaaS, technology, or product-driven organisations.
* A solid understanding of product design, UX, interaction design, and user-centred methods.
* Experience building and improving scalable design processes, systems, and governance.
* Experience collaborating with Product Management and Engineering teams in cross-functional environments.
* Experience developing designers through coaching, feedback, and regular 1:1s.
* Comfortable leading a distributed team across different locations and time zones.
* Clear communication and stakeholder skills, with the ability to build alignment across teams and organisational levels.
* Ability to balance strategic thinking with operational execution in fast-moving environments.
* Good organisational skills and the ability to manage several projects at once.
* High proficiency in English, both written and verbal.
* Fluent Swedish, both written and spoken.
* Swedish citizenship, because this role supports defence work and the successful candidate must pass Swedish security vetting (säkerhetsprövning).

**Desirable:**

* Experience working with complex B2B platforms or mission-critical software environments.
* Familiarity with design systems and scalable product design practices.
* Experience working in international or multi-disciplinary organisations.
* Experience supporting organisational change and design maturity growth.
* Formal education or training within UX, interaction design, service design, or related disciplines.


You do not need to tick every box. If you meet most of these points and are excited about the role, we would love to hear from you. A few requirements for this role — such as Swedish citizenship and security vetting — come only from the security rules for defence work, not from personal preference.

**What we offer**

* Occupational pension (tjänstepension).
* Wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag).
* Healthcare insurance & healthcare insurance for treatment
* Parental pay
* Vaccinations
* Room to grow into a Director-level design leadership role over time.
* A supportive, collaborative team building meaningful products for mission-critical work.

**About 4C Strategies**


4C Strategies is a global provider of readiness and resilience solutions, supporting organisations across defence, public sector, corporate, and critical infrastructure environments. Through our Exonaut platform and expert services, we help organisations train, exercise, evaluate, and improve their operational capability and preparedness.


At 4C, we combine technology, expertise, and collaboration to solve complex challenges in mission-critical environments. Our teams work closely together across disciplines to create user-focused solutions that strengthen resilience and operational performance worldwide.

**How to apply**


To apply, click the Apply today button. We review applications on a rolling basis, so please apply as soon as you can.

title: Founding UI/UX & Brand Designer
description:
TL;DR
  
We're looking for a Founding Designer to own how Spiich looks, feels, and behaves — across product and brand. You'll define the UI/UX for every surface (web, mobile, browser extension) and shape the visual identity of the company itself. Production React/vibe coding skills are a plus, not a requirement — taste, craft, and judgment are the job.
  
Why join
  
We're a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value speed, rigour, and extreme ownership. If you're obsessed with craft, thrive in ambiguity, and want to define how sales professionals experience AI, you'll feel at home.
  
**Design at Spiich is an open field:** agentic software has no established patterns yet. How should a rep review what an autonomous agent did overnight? What does trust look like in an interface? You'll answer questions most designers never get to ask.
  
We're backed by founders and operators behind Lovable, OpenAI, Tandem Health, and Neo4j. Our customers across 10 countries report 90% less prospecting time, 3x market coverage, and 2x customer meetings.
  
What you'll do (responsibilities)
  
**In one sentence:** Own design end-to-end — product UX and company brand.
  
**Design the product across web, mobile, and browser extension:** flows, wireframes, prototypes, and final UI
  
Invent interaction patterns for agentic software — reviewing agent work, voice-first flows, and trust and transparency in AI actions
  
Build and own the design system our engineers ship from
  
**Prototype at the speed of thought:** use AI tools to go from idea to working demo in hours, not sprints
  
**Own the brand:** visual identity, website, decks, and everything the world sees — and shape the next chapter of our brand as we grow
  
Do user research with real customers, iterate daily, and let findings kill your darlings
  
Work shoulder-to-shoulder with engineers so designs ship fast and ship true
  
  

Who you are
  
Designer with exceptional taste and a portfolio that proves it — both product work and brand/visual work
  
**Strong in UX thinking and UI craft:** typography, spacing, motion, and the details most people miss
  
Proficient in Figma; comfortable prototyping at high fidelity
  
Comfortable with ambiguity, early-stage chaos, and fast iteration
  
Care more about solving problems than polishing abstractions
  
**Fully committed to the mission:** not "just a job"
  
  

**Bonus:** You prototype and ship with AI tools — Lovable, Claude Design, Claude Code, Codex, or similar. Production React is welcome too, but we have engineers for that.
  
What we offer
  
A team of obsessed builders, full of speed, rigour, and grit
  
**Full design ownership:** no design-by-committee, no layers between you and shipped product
  
**Mission-driven, no-BS culture:** experiment, ship, learn
  
Meaningful equity through qualified employee stock options (QESO) from day one
  
Fully on-site in Stockholm - we believe in working together
  
Top-tier compensation package
  
  

Our tools
  
**Design:** Figma
  
AI: Lovable, Claude Design, Claude Code, Codex — use whatever makes you fastest
  
**Product:** React, React Native, TypeScript — built by our engineers, with you if you code
  
**Platforms:** Web app, mobile (iOS & Android), browser extension
  
  

About Spiich Labs
  
Spiich is the agentic platform for sales teams. Reps focus on the human work — building relationships, building trust, and closing deals — while Spiich agents handle everything else: prospecting, lead generation, CRM updates, meeting prep, follow-ups, and everything in between. We're backed by founders and operators behind Lovable, OpenAI, Tandem Health, and Neo4j.
  
How we hire
  
Submit your application
  
Intro call with the team
  
**Portfolio deep-dive:** walk us through work you're proud of and the decisions behind it
  
Design exercise
  
Join us for trial work lasting 2 days on-site. We'll see how you tick and you get to meet the team and explore whether joining Spiich feels right for you.
  
  

We move fast — the full process typically takes under two weeks.
  
How to Apply
  
Drop us a line at filip@spiich.ai with your portfolio, a note on why this excites you, and your proudest work. No CV needed.

title: Lead SAP Software Engineer
description:
**Job Description** **WHAT YOU’LL DO**


As a **Lead SAP Software Engineer**, you will lead the engineering direction of our master data team, building and modernizing the master data solutions which are the backbone of H&M's SAP landscape.


You will provide technical leadership, define scalable solutions and architectures, mentor engineers across Stockholm and Bengaluru, and drive engineering excellence through modern development practices in **SAP ABAP, CPI, BTP** etc. You will be working closely with Product Managers, Enterprise Architects, and business stakeholders, you will deliver secure, resilient, and high-performing master data solutions that enable critical business operations.

**WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH**


You will collaborate with Software Engineers, Product Managers, Enterprise Architects, Scrum Masters, and business stakeholders across global teams in Stockholm and Bengaluru. Together, you'll define technical roadmap of SAP master data solutions.

**WHO YOU ARE**


We are looking for people with…

* 7+ years of software engineering experience, including experience in a Technical Lead or Staff Engineer role.
* Strong experience in **SAP ABAP Development**, Object-Oriented ABAP, design patterns, clean coding principles, reusable frameworks, and enterprise application architecture.
* Strong experience in **SAP BTP Architecture**, including the ability to architect composable, event-driven, and API-based solutions across SAP BTP and on-premises systems.
* Strong experience in **Clean Core implementation**, SAP Application Extension Methodology, ABAP Cloud, In-App Extensibility, and Side-by-Side Extensions.
* Strong experience in **ABAP CDS**, Code Pushdown, HANA Modelling, and performance optimization for effective data modelling and database management.
* Strong expertise in SAP BTP RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model), OData services, API development, and modern ABAP development practices.
* Strong hands-on experience with classical ABAP concepts including Reports, ALV, Data Dictionary, Function Modules, BAPIs, RFCs, IDocs, Enhancements, User Exits, Customer Exits, BAdIs, and interface development.
* Strong troubleshooting and ABAP debugging skills, including experience with complex production support scenarios, update debugging, background debugging, RFC debugging, SQL Trace (ST05), Runtime Analysis (SAT), performance tuning, and root-cause analysis.
* Proficiency in **unit testing with ABAP Unit**, conducting code reviews, enforcing development standards, ensuring maintainable and high-quality code, and driving continuous improvement initiatives.
* Ability to mentor development teams, review technical designs, provide architecture guidance, and drive adoption of SAP best practices across projects and programs.


And people who are responsible for…

* To provide simple, efficient and scalable **E-2-E SAP solutions** primarily in SAP ECC, SAP MDG, SAP CRM systems which enables us to ensure reliable data of highest quality to all consuming applications.
* To provide quick and efficient **Master Data Maintenance services** and Roll Out of Master Data Solutions in Production systems primarily in SAP ECC, SAP MDG, SAP CRM systems.
* To design & develop New Information-Steward (IS) Reports and generate periodic IS reports for teams having Data monitoring requirements.


Added Advantage to know..

* Hands-on experience with SAP Integration Suite, CPI, API Management, Event Mesh, Solace, Kafka, and event-driven integration architectures.
* Knowledge of SAP Fiori, SAPUI5, Fiori Elements, Gateway, and modern SAP UX technologies.

**WHO WE ARE**


H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.


We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future.

**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE**


At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer attractive benefits, flexible ways of working, and extensive opportunities for professional growth across our global organization.


You'll work on business-critical enterprise platforms, collaborate with talented global engineering teams, and help shape the future of supply chain technology using modern cloud, integration, and AI-driven engineering practices.

**JOIN US**


Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.


Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.
company_description: At H&M, we welcome you to be yourself and challenge you to grow and make a difference. Help us reimagine the future of an entire industry, making everyone look, feel & do good.

title: Gruppchef inom applikationsutveckling
description:
Om Kriminalvården Kriminalvården växer – vi bygger nytt, bygger om och bygger till och söker nu fler medarbetare. Vårt viktigaste mål är att minska återfall i brott – att bryta den onda cirkeln. Lyckas vi med det blir samhället tryggare och säkrare för alla. Vi är den myndighet som ansvarar för anstalter, frivård, häkten och klienttransporter.


Vill du vara med och bidra till ett tryggare samhälle? Vår IT-avdelning genomför en stor satsning med fokus på en digital Kriminalvård. Tillsammans jobbar vi för att skapa förutsättningar och lösningar som gör skillnad i vår verksamhet. IT-avdelningen är under stark tillväxt. Vi söker dig som har ett stort IT-intresse, kunskaper och erfarenheter som kan berika vår digitaliseringsresa och som vill vara med och göra skillnad i samhället inom just din profession.

### **Arbetsbeskrivning**


Som chef på IT avdelningen har du ett samhällsviktigt uppdrag och du får vara med i myndighetens resa mot en mer digital kriminalvård. Du blir en del av den enhet som arbetar med digitalisering för Kriminalvårdens kärnverksamhet. I ditt ansvarsområde har du ett helhetsansvar för personal, verksamhet och budget. Gruppen består av 10–15 anställda plus konsulter, som tillsammans utgör upp till 23 personer över tid. Gruppen jobbar i en komplex och utmanande IT-miljö där arbetet kräver ett behovsdrivet förhållningssätt i tät samverkan med mottagande verksamhet och dess slutanvändare.
  

  

Som gruppchef kommer du att leda och vidareutveckla en grupp som ansvarar för att utveckla och förvalta applikationer. Vi arbetar i utvecklingsteam som är bemannade med olika roller så som Systemutvecklare, Arkitekt, Testare, Kravanalytiker och UX-Designers. Gruppen ansvarar även för att säkerställa en tillgänglig, säker och kostnadseffektiv IT- leverans.
  

  

Du är en förebild som engagerar och tar tillvara medarbetarnas initiativ. Du ser individen, identifierar varje individs inre motivation och skapar engagemang. Du strävar efter att skapa psykologiskt trygghet inom gruppen. I rollen som gruppchef ansvarar du för gruppens arbete med kontinuerliga förbättrings- och förändringsarbeten och du är delaktig i att skapa en attraktiv arbetsplats med god arbetsmiljö. Tillsammans med dina chefskollegor arbetar du i sektionens- samt enhetens ledningsgrupp för att planera och följa upp verksamhetens utveckling.

### **Kvalifikationer**


Vi söker dig som är en stabil och trygg ledare. Du är prestigelös, initiativtagande och flexibel i ditt agerande och har lätt för att initiera förändringsprocesser vilket leder till framdrift och resultat. Vidare har du en god problemlösnings- och planeringsförmåga. Du kan bryta ner och analysera mål, tydliggöra dem för medarbetare och samtidigt leda, motivera och engagera för att säkerställa att ni gemensamt och effektivt jobbar framåt. Slutligen är du en relationsbyggare och du har lätt för att både skapa och underhålla kontakter. I din kommunikation är du tydlig och lyhörd och du säkerställer att budskap når fram och att förväntningar är tydliga för alla berörda parter.


För oss är det viktigt att du delar Kriminalvårdens grundläggande värderingar om en human människosyn, respekt för individen samt tron på människans vilja och förmåga att utvecklas. Du är även medveten om och har förståelse för hur bakgrund, kultur och grupptillhörighet påverkar dig själv och andra. Vidare förväntas du med ditt förhållningssätt bidra till att alla blir likvärdigt bemötta. Vi lägger stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet.

#### **Vi söker dig som har:**

* Akademisk examen inom relevant ämnesområde, såsom systemvetare, dataingenjör eller liknande alternativt annan utbildning i kombination med erfarenhet som Kriminalvården bedömer relevant
* Erfarenhet av att vara chef med personal- samt budgetansvar inom IT-området, eller annan ledarerfarenhet som vi bedömer är relevant
* Erfarenhet av att driva förändringsarbete
* Erfarenhet av systemutveckling eller upphandling av IT system
* Erfarenhet av offentlig verksamhet eller större komplexa organisationer
* Erfarenhet av Agila arbetssätt
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig väl i tal och skrift på såväl svenska som engelska

#### **Det är även meriterande med:**

* Dokumenterad ledarskapsutbildning
* Erfarenhet av projektledning
* God kunskap om- samt erfarenhet av praktisk tillämpning av tillitsbaserat ledarskap


Övrigt Kriminalvården har valt att ta bort det personliga brevet och ersatt det med urvalsfrågor som är relevanta för tjänsten. Svaren i urvalsfrågorna och CV ligger till grund för om du är kvalificerad att gå vidare i processen. För att bli aktuell för anställning inom Kriminalvården krävs att du motsvarar högt ställda krav gällande lämplighet, omdöme, säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetande och att du följer gällande lagar. Det innebär att du står upp för demokratiska värderingar och den statliga värdegrund som gäller för Kriminalvårdens verksamhet. Vissa befattningar är placerade i säkerhetsklass och då kommer en säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll att genomföras innan beslut om anställning fattas. För vissa säkerhetsklassade tjänster krävs svenskt medborgarskap. Kriminalvården välkomnar sökande med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter. Läs mer om säkerhetsprövning här: https://www.kriminalvarden.se/jobba-hos-oss/rekryteringsprocessen/sakerhetsprovning-vid-rekrytering/ Befattningen kan innebära krigsplacering vid Kriminalvården. Läs mer om krigsplacering här: https://www.kriminalvarden.se/jobba-hos-oss/rekryteringsprocessen/krigsplacering/ Individuell lönesättning tillämpas. Provanställning kan komma att tillämpas. Kriminalvården strävar efter en jämn könsfördelning och ökad mångfald. För att skicka in din ansökan, klicka på ansökningslänken i annonsen. Frågor om hur du ansöker och lägger in ditt CV ställer du direkt till Varbi. Frågor om specifika jobb besvaras av den kontaktperson som anges i annonsen. Vi undanber oss vänligen men bestämt direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt försäljare av rekryteringsannonser. Kriminalvården har upphandlade avtal.

title: Product Designer
description:
**YOUR MISSION AT VOI** 




 Voi is making it easier to get around cities without a car. We build shared e-scooters, e-bikes and the software that powers them — from the rider app to the tools our field teams and warehouses use every day.
 



 As a Product Designer, you will join a cross-functional team and own the experience of our products from discovery through to delivery. You will lead projects end-to-end, bringing a clear point of view, collaborating closely with your Product Manager and engineering team, and helping shape products that work for riders and cities alike.
 



 You will take an insight-led approach, connect design decisions to business outcomes and proactively turn opportunities into meaningful improvements
 


* Lead discovery and design for your team’s projects, from problem framing to shipped product
* Frame hypotheses, explore a range of solutions and converge on clear, well-reasoned decisions
* Plan and conduct user research, using qualitative and quantitative data to inform design decisions and evaluate impact
* Advocate for a high-quality, accessible and consistent user experience across Voi’s products
* Contribute to roadmap planning, strategic discussions, ideation, user testing, implementation and feedback loops
* Partner closely with Product, Engineering and other stakeholders to bring solutions to life
* Contribute to design team rituals, processes and ways of working that strengthen design maturity across Voi
* Explore new methods, analytics, metrics and AI tools, and apply what you learn in practice



  





**WHAT YOU NEED TO EMBARK** 



* You are a thoughtful and proactive designer who enjoys working in fast-moving, cross-functional teams.
* You balance curiosity with clear decision-making, communicate your reasoning confidently and care about creating useful experiences grounded in real user needs.
* At least three years’ experience in product or UX design within cross-functional teams.
* Experience leading the design process from discovery and problem definition through to delivery and iteration.
* Ability to independently plan and run user research, and translate insights into design decisions.
* Experience using qualitative insights, analytics and product metrics to assess and improve your work.
* Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to explain design decisions to a range of stakeholders.
* A collaborative working style, alongside the confidence to take ownership and work independently.
* Comfort using AI tools as part of your everyday design and research process.
* Professional proficiency in English.
* It would be great if you also have experience working with hardware or firmware, building and maintaining design systems, or conducting exploratory research beyond usability testing and validation.



  





 Don’t meet every requirement? If you are excited about this role and our mission, and have relevant, transferable skills, we encourage you to apply – even if you don’t check every box.
 



  





**WHY VOI?** 




 Working at Voi is more than just a job; our People Promise includes a personal voyage where you will grow as a professional and be a part of a team and culture that builds something meaningful for society. In addition to this you’ll have the opportunity to:
 


* Join Europe’s #1 micromobility operator and one of the fastest growing scale-ups.
* Work with a supportive Product Design team of seven colleagues across diverse nationalities.
* Help shape the direction of sustainable urban transport and products used across Europe.
* Get “skin in the game” through our employee options programme.
* Enjoy unlimited free Voi rides and a dog-friendly office.
* Work from our central Stockholm office on Sveavägen, with flexible working hours and an expectation of three office days per week.
* Receive 30 days of paid holiday, a SEK 3,000 annual wellness allowance and the equipment you need to do your best work.
* Work together with inspiring, motivated and fun colleagues towards a common goal: creating sustainable cities made for living, free from noise and pollution.

title: UX Designer
description:
We are on a mission to become the leading international force in residential property management. What we do matters to billions of people in their everyday lives.
   

  

 Residential real estate is the world’s largest asset class, and for most families, buying a home is the single biggest financial decision they will ever make. For too long, residential property management has fallen short of homeowners' and residents' expectations, and the industry is ready for change. Odevo was formed to make that change happen.
   

  

 In just six years, Odevo has grown more than 70-fold, establishing us as the leading international player. We are more than 12,000 people across the US, the UK, the Nordics, Germany, Spain, Portugal, Mexico, and Italy building Odevo. Together, we aspire to break new ground through innovation and cultivate a workplace where we help each other succeed, where ideas matter more than titles, and where kindness is a strength.
   

  

 By combining the power of people and technology, we set a new standard for how homes are managed. Our technology-enabled services simplify living for residents and create a better experience for property owners and boards.
   

  

 Our ambition is to continue our fast-growing journey through new customers, service expansion, and by partnering with great companies that share our mindset and ambition, both in existing and new markets.
   

  

 As we continue to grow, we stay true to who we are, challenge unnecessary bureaucracy, and keep decision-making close to our customers.
   

  

 If you share our mindset and ambition, we’d love you to join us and help accelerate our mission together.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We’re looking for a UX Designer with strong AI experience to help shape not only the products we design, but also how design is practiced, specified, scaled, and implemented across our global product organisation.
   

  

 This is a unique opportunity to join an industry still in an early stage of digital maturity, where you can have a significant impact. We see customer experience as a critical differentiator and an important part of our ambition to transform the global property management industry.
   

  

 You’ll join a global design team led by the Head of CX & UX in Stockholm, Sweden. This role combines hands-on UX and interaction design in our AI team with helping the wider design team scale quality, consistency, design systems, and AI-assisted product development workflows globally. You will collaborate with the global design team and support knowledge sharing across all our markets, including the UK, Europe, the US, and Mexico. As part of this role, you will shape both intuitive customer-facing experiences and complex internal workflows for property management employees.
   

  

 You will help mature how our design team works across markets, products, and disciplines. This includes improving design system structure, documentation, governance, and adoption; defining practical quality standards; and establishing clearer ways of working across Figma, design specifications, no-code tools, and AI-assisted development workflows.
   

  

 AI will be central to how we evolve our design practice going forward, balancing the efficiency of AI agents with the judgement, taste, and craft of experienced designers. Experience in both DesignOps and AI-assisted design and development will therefore be a strong advantage.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Work and lead the “ordinary” design process within product teams - translating user needs, business goals, and technical constraints into clear flows, prototypes, interaction design to test and learn iteratively.
* Define design practices for agent driven development workflows, and how design artefacts should be specified so they can be used effectively by designers, product managers, engineers, and agents. Eg specification formats, reusable prompts, design skills, MCPs, and validation methods.
* Define how design artefacts — such as user flows, service blueprints, prototypes, component specifications, interaction rules, and acceptance criteria — can better steer and validate AI-assisted implementation.
* Support more efficient research and validation practices, like how we evaluate product iterations and AI-generated outputs against user needs, design system standards, accessibility expectations, and product quality criteria. This includes, for example, defining/creating tools for research planning, recruitment processes, GDPR considerations, research templates, and maintaining a research repository.
* Lead the work with the Odevo design system(s) by improving component documentation, governance, accessibility standards, usage guidance, and alignment between design components/patterns and code.


**Who You Are**
 You are genuinely interested in understanding people’s needs, behaviours, and motivations, and you use that insight to shape services that deliver real value. You combine strong design craft with systems thinking and can move comfortably between user needs, business outcomes, service journeys, and detailed interaction design.
   

  

 You strive for quality, value collaboration over prestige, and enjoy contributing to a strong team culture. You are a strong communicator who can turn research insights and design rationale into shared understanding across teams and stakeholders.
   

  

 You enjoy making design work more legible, scalable, and repeatable. You care about the craft of design, but also about the systems, rituals, documentation, and standards that help teams deliver good design consistently. You are comfortable moving between hands-on product design and improving the underlying ways of working that support the wider design organisation.
   

  

 You are pragmatic about AI: curious and experimental, but also thoughtful about quality, trust, transparency, human oversight, and when AI should or should not be used.
   

  

**Experience**
* 5+ years of experience as a UX Designer, Product Designer, or similar role, working on complex digital products.
* Bachelor’s degree in design, HCI, behavioural science, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
* Strong proficiency in Figma, prototyping, interaction design, UX research, service design, and design systems.
* Experience designing web and mobile products for complex, data-rich, workflow-heavy, or operational environments.
* Experience working in agile, cross-functional teams with product managers, engineers, designers, and stakeholders.
* Experience contributing to DesignOps and design systems, including documentation, governance, quality standards, tooling, ResearchOps, and alignment between design and code.
* Experience with AI-driven design or development workflows, including how structured specifications, component rules, interaction logic, and acceptance criteria can guide AI-assisted implementation.
* Experience designing AI-enabled product experiences, such as intelligent search, automation, recommendations, copilots, or decision-support tools.
* Property management or proptech experience is a plus.


**What We Offer**
* Professional growth - to work with the most talented developers in the industry.
* Modern technology - we invest in the latest technologies and tools and encourage our team members to share their ideas and take ownership of their work.
* Innovation - to work on exciting projects that push the boundaries of our industry and make a real impact.
* Commitment to quality - a dynamic and forward-thinking company that values profitable and long-term product development.


**Sounds like you?** 
 Apply today!
   

  

 #msl
   

  

 From 30 K to 2,2 million homes under Management in 5 years
   

  

 year est.
   

  

 Homes
   

  

 colleagues
   

  

 Markets
   

  

 property owners

title: UX Researcher
description:
**TL;DR –** 
 We’re looking for a curious, fast-moving UX Researcher to help shape the future of Lovable. You’ll uncover how users think, feel, and act, turning insights into product direction. You’ll design studies, test concepts, and experiment with new AI-assisted ways of learning from users, helping us move fast without compromising on craft.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Lovable-built applications and websites are visited hundreds of millions of times a month, and our enterprise footprint is compounding fast. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
 We’re looking for a researcher who balances
 **curiosity and rigor** 
 — someone who loves asking
 *why* 
 , tests
 *how* 
 , and measures
 *what’s working* 
 . You’re as comfortable in a user interview as you are interpreting event data or survey results, and you know how to connect human stories with product metrics.
   

  

* 3–6 years of experience in UX research, human-centered design, or a related role.
* Proficiency in qualitative methods (interviews, usability testing, concept testing).
* Experience with survey design, data analysis, and working with behavioral data (e.g., from analytics tools) is a plus
* Strong synthesis skills, distilling findings into insights that influence design and product decisions.
* Excellent communication and storytelling skills to bring research to life for cross-functional teams.
* Comfortable in fast-paced, iterative environments with shifting priorities.
* Experience working closely with designers, PMs, and data scientists.


**What You’ll Do**
 In one sentence:
   

  

**Turn user insights into product direction that shapes how the world builds software.**
* Partner with product, design, growth and data teams to define learning goals for upcoming features and experiments.
* Explore new AI-driven approaches for recruiting, analysis, and synthesis — expanding how we learn from users.
* Conduct user sessions directly with Lovable users, gathering firsthand insights into behaviors, motivations, and unmet needs.
* Collaborate with data scientists to interpret behavioral data and validate qualitative findings.
* Contribute to building research frameworks, templates, and best practices for scalable discovery at Lovable.


**How We Hire**
* Fill out a short form and hop on a quick intro call with our recruiting team
* Meet designers and PMs to discuss your approach to research
* Walk through past work and real examples of your process
* Final conversations with product leadership


**About Your Application**
 Please submit your application in English — it’s our company language.
   

  

 We treat all candidates equally, so please apply through our careers portal.

title: Digital Product Designer
description:
**Role: Sr. Digital Product Designer** 




**Location: Stockholm, SE** 




**Type: Contract** 




  





 Job Description:
 


* Experience in digital product design and innovation across web, mobile applications, and internal tools within large-scale organizations.
* Exceptional storytelling and narrative-building skills to communicate ideas with clarity and influence.
* Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to navigate ambiguity and align diverse stakeholders.
* A self-driven mindset with proven experience leading design initiatives from strategy through execution.
* Proficiency in industry-standard design and collaboration tools such as Figma, Miro, and presentation software.
* Is motivated by creating meaningful value for both users and the business.
* Stays curious, humble, and informed about emerging trends in digital experiences, retail, and AI.

title: Product Designer
description:
**What We Do At Qliro**
 At Qliro we deliver safe and simple payments. We are a dedicated partner to our merchants who serve their customers every day. We believe that if our merchants grow, we grow. We are a company of dedicated, friendly and highly competent people that together have redefined what Qliro and payments can be. We are now on a journey of scaling our business both here in Sweden and the Nordics.
   

  

 What your teams are doing:
   

  

 We are seeking a Product Designer to join our design team, working closely with two of our product teams: Checkout Experience and Web & App. As a Product Designer at Qliro you own the full design cycle — research, prototyping, and crafting engaging, intuitive experiences — across the products these two teams build. You work side by side with Product Managers and engineers in both teams to drive the design forward based on research and data, and you help shape our shared design framework together with the rest of the design team.
   

  

* Checkout Experience owns the checkout flow for our merchants’ customers, building the best e-commerce checkout and payment widgets. It’s a fullstack team with a lot of humour, collaboration and drive.
* Web & App builds the products where our merchants’ consumers follow their purchases. A self-organised, autonomous team with full ownership of features from discovery all the way to operating them in production — fullstack, with most of its focus on the front end.


 What you will get to do:
   

  

* Drive the end-to-end design process, from discovery to implementation, across the Checkout Experience and Web & App product areas
* Break down complex UX and design challenges into safe, simple and tangible solutions
* Partner closely with Product and engineering in both teams from an early stage — understanding technical limitations and keeping consumer value at the centre
* Balance the needs of two product teams, prioritising where your design time creates the most impact
* Make AI part of how you work — using it to move faster, explore more directions and raise the quality of what you ship. At Qliro we expect everyone to actively embrace AI in their craft
* Help define our design framework and ways of working together with the other designers in the design team
* Help grow an insight-driven design and product culture at Qliro


 What you'll bring to the team
   

  

 You are a talented Product Designer with a creative mindset on how to solve tasks efficiently. You identify areas for improvement and can work both along a long-term strategic plan and deliver quick fixes where needed. You’re comfortable serving more than one team at once — juggling different contexts, stakeholders and priorities without losing the thread. You have extensive experience with digital products in fast-moving environments and a good understanding of the financial world and the demands it places on a great user experience.
   

  

* Relevant education and 3–7 years of experience in product design, preferably within a payments domain
* Figma is your best friend
* Attention to detail and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment
* Strong collaboration skills, a real team player
* Experience within the financial industry, or a good understanding of financial systems through interest
* Naturally curious and insights/data driven
* Can create structure in chaos by being self-driven and action oriented
* Strong proficiency in Swedish, both writing and speech


 WOW in everything we do
   

  

 Qliro is the place for you who are curious, passionate and love collaboration. Together we have the power to create wow in everything we do. Feel comfortable being you and bringing your own unique perspectives. At Qliro there is a place for you whoever you are — differences are a strength.
   

  

 We love flexibility and remote work, and we also believe in the power of meeting in real life. With a mix of working remotely and collaborating in the office, we believe in a flexible workplace that suits both you and Qliro.
   

  

 Does this sound like your next role? What are you waiting for - apply now!
   

  

*Qliro is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to gender, gender identity or expression, national origin, religion or other beliefs, disability, sexual* 
 orientation
 *or age* 
 .

title: Senior Product Designer
description:
Are you a product designer shaping best-in-class podcast experiences?
   

  

 What if your design work didn’t just improve interfaces, but changed how people build habits and keep coming back?
   

  

 At Podme, success isn’t measured by what we ship. It’s measured by whether people return, build habits, and stay. That means design isn’t just about screens it’s about helping users succeed in ways that drive real business outcomes.
   

  

 We’re looking for a Senior Product Designer (UX) who wants to design at that level of impact.
   

  

**About The Role**
 This is not a role for someone who polishes interfaces after decisions are already made.
   

  

 This is a role for someone who helps shape what gets built, by understanding user behavior, identifying the right problems, and designing experiences that create meaningful outcomes for both listeners and the business.
   

  

 At Podme, great design helps people discover content they love, continue listening effortlessly, and build habits around premium podcasts and audio experiences.
   

  

 What you’ll do
   

  

****As a Senior Product Designer (UX), You Will:****
* Design intuitive and delightful mobile experiences across iOS and Android
* Create seamless listening and viewing experiences around premium podcasts
* Frame product opportunities through user behavior, motivation, and outcomes
* Work closely with Product Managers, Engineers, Data Analysts, and stakeholders across the company
* Use research, experimentation, and data to validate decisions and improve experiences continuously
* Help shape product direction, not just interface execution


 You’ll be especially focused on
   

  

**Designing for habits and continuation**
**You’ll Design Experiences That:**
* Reduce friction and cognitive load
* Help users discover content they genuinely care about
* Make continuation feel effortless across moments and devices
* Create clear and coherent journeys across listening and discovery
* Make Podme feel like a natural part of everyday routines


**Framing the right problems**
**You’ll:**
* Use research and behavioral data to identify where the experience breaks down
* Collaborate with stakeholders and subject matter experts to understand opportunities and constraints
* Turn insights into clear product direction, hypotheses, and design principles
* Focus on user outcomes before interface solutions


**Driving continuous discovery and learning**
**You’ll:**
* Participate actively in research and insight work
* Prototype and validate ideas early
* Run research experiments and iterate based on real behavior, not assumptions
* Help teams learn faster and make better product decisions


****Designing For Clarity, Trust, And Premium Experiences****
 You’ll help create experiences where:
   

  

* Navigation and continuation feel effortless
* Users feel confident in their choices
* The value of premium content is consistently clear
* Audio and video experiences feel seamless across contexts and devices


**Working as a true product partner**
 You’ll collaborate closely with Product Managers and Engineers to:
   

  

* Identify friction and opportunities
* Prioritize the most meaningful problems
* Validate outcomes through experiments and behavioral metrics
* Ensure experiences are scalable, feasible, and impactful


 Together, you share accountability for user outcomes, not just delivery.
   

  

**Contributing to a stronger design culture**
**You’ll:**
* Raise the bar for UX thinking, systems design, and product craft
* Help evolve how we design adaptive and AI-supported experiences
* Contribute to a culture where design drives measurable impact, not just output


**This position will report to the UX Manager at Podme.**
 What success looks like
   

  

* Your work leads to measurable improvements in how people experience Podme
* Teams focus on solving the right problems, not just shipping more features
* User needs and trade-offs become clearer, helping teams make better decisions faster
* You turn research and behavioral insights into clear product direction
* The experiences you design consistently increase repeat listening and habit formation
* You become a trusted partner in shaping product strategy and experience direction
* You help strengthen a product culture where design is deeply connected to business impact


 Who you are
   

  

* You have strong experience in product design for mobile products (iOS and Android)
* You think in systems, flows, and user journeys, not just individual screens
* You are comfortable working with ambiguity and helping shape direction
* You use research and data to inform and challenge decisions
* You collaborate naturally across disciplines and involve stakeholders early
* You care deeply about user outcomes, product quality, and business impact


 Bonus points if you have
   

  

* Experience with audio, video, or subscription products
* Experience designing recommendation or discovery experiences
* Experience working with AI-driven or adaptive systems
* Strong understanding of behavioral metrics and retention


****Who We Are****
 Podme is the place for premium podcasts and audiobooks. With over 350,000 paying subscribers, we’re available in Sweden, Norway, and Finland, and part of Schibsted Media since 2021.
   

  

 Our vision is to enrich people’s lives through stories, which embodies our core belief in the power of narratives to bring joy, meaning, and connection to individuals. We strive to create effortless and habit-forming audio and product experiences that resonate with the listener and add value in their everyday lives.
   

  

 We’re a friendly and passionate team of +80 people, here to make listening effortless, inspiring, and part of everyday life. Backed by Schibsted Media, the largest media group in Scandinavia, we are now on a growth journey which requires us to strengthen (example: the data-driven excellence in our company.

title: Senior Product Designer
description:
Vill du bygga framtidens nyhetsupplevelse tillsammans med oss?
   

  

 Omni söker en Senior Product Designer som vill arbeta nära produkt, UX och utveckling för att skapa smartare, snabbare och bättre nyhetsupplevelser. Det här är en roll för dig som drivs av möjligheten att använda AI, stark produktdesign och ny teknik för att hjälpa människor att förstå vad som händer i världen – på ett sätt som är mer relevant, personligt och anpassat efter deras behov.
   

  

 Hos oss får du arbeta med en av Sveriges starkaste nyhetsprodukter, i ett team där idéer snabbt kan bli verklighet. Om du vill jobba med nyheter på en plats där produktutveckling, användarupplevelse och AI på riktigt kan förändra hur människor konsumerar och förstår nyheter, är Omni där du vill vara.
   

  

 Det här är en roll för dig som gillar att få saker att hända. Du är både strukturerad och hands-on, trivs i gränslandet mellan produkt, design och teknik och tycker om att lösa problem tillsammans med andra.
   

  

 Om rollen
   

  

 Du kommer att arbeta tätt tillsammans med Head of Product, Head of UX och utvecklingsteamet i det dagliga produktarbetet. Tjänsten är en projektanställning på 12 månader med god möjlighet till förlängning.
   

  

 Rollen är bred och operativ med stort fokus på genomförande, problemlösning och samarbete.
   

  

**Exempel På Arbetsuppgifter**
* Driva och projektleda mindre produktinitiativ tillsammans med andra teammedlemmar
* Ta fram skisser och wireframes i Figma utifrån användarbehov, affärsbehov och tekniska möjligheter
* Arbeta nära utvecklare för att förtydliga lösningar och säkerställa bra leverans
* Vara med och definiera produktkrav, flöden och affärsregler
* Översätta användarbehov och organisatoriska behov till konkreta produktlösningar
* Hjälpa till att strukturera och driva arbete framåt
* Bidra till utvecklingen av våra produkter, processer och arbetssätt
* Utforska hur AI och ny teknik kan förbättra våra produkter och användarupplevelser


 Vi tror att du
   

  

* Är en stark problemlösare som gillar att ta ansvar och driva arbete framåt
* Är operativ och trivs med att vara hands-on
* Har erfarenhet av produktutveckling i digitala produkter och appar
* Har gedigen erfarenhet av UX- och UI-arbete och är bekant med relevanta verktyg såsom Figma
* Är van att arbeta nära utvecklare
* Tänker ur ett helhetsperspektiv utifrån användarbehov, affärsmål och tekniska förutsättningar
* Är nyfiken på AI, nya verktyg och modern produktutveckling
* Kommunicerar tydligt och samarbetar bra med olika typer av kompetenser på svenska såväl engelska


 Det är ett extra plus om du är intresserad av media, nyheter eller innehållsdrivna produkter.
   

  

 Hos Omni
   

  

 På Omni arbetar vi med produkter som används av flera hundratusentals människor varje vecka. Vi är ett litet och snabbfotat team där idéer snabbt kan bli verklighet och där produkt, UX och teknik arbetar nära varandra.
   

  

 Hos Omni är alla medarbetares insatser, kunskap och idéer lika mycket värda. Det har skapat en stark, prestigelös och samarbetsdriven kultur där människor hjälper varandra och där bra idéer kan komma från vem som helst.
   

  

 Entreprenörsandan är viktig för oss och bidrar till en utvecklande och stimulerande arbetsmiljö där det finns stort utrymme att testa nytt, ta initiativ och påverka både produkterna och hur vi arbetar framåt.

title: Product Manager, User Experience
description:
**An Amazing Career Opportunity for a Product Manager User Experience!!**
**Location: Stockholm, Sweden / Suresnes, France / Cardiff, Wales / Krakow, Poland**
**Job ID: 42038**
 The Product Manager for User Experience will be responsible for mapping all user touch points across all categories of readers and translating these into a cohesive user experience.This will require revisiting the entire software tools & SDK product portfolio and establishing a class leading implementation strategy, facilitating the easy integration of our embedded products, as well as the desktop readers and handheld devices.
   

  

 This role comprises not only owning all cloud, desktop and mobile tools & SDKs but also our diverse Cloud APIs and services that help customers to integrate and manage our readers within their solutions. The PM will need to work closely with various teams, including Engineering, Sales, Pre-Sales, Marketing, and Operations, to successfully execute the defined strategies and meet business and market requirements.
   

  

 Are you a problem solver, an out-of-the-box thinker, and a knowledge seeker? If the answer is “yes,” this mission is for you. HID is seeking a new Product Manager for revitalizing the user experience within the RFID Reader division.
   

  

**Who are we?**
 HID powers the trusted identities of the world’s people, places, and things, allowing people to transact safely, work productively and travel freely.
   

  

 We are a high-tech software company headquartered in Austin, TX, with over 4,500 worldwide employees. Check us out here: www.hidglobal.com and https://youtu.be/23km5H4K9Eo
   

  

**Identification Technologies (IDT):**
 IDT is creating trusted identities for IoT applications through Smart Components and Enabling Services.
   

  

 HID’s Identification Technologies powers the trusted identities of the world’s people, places and things through smart components and cloud services. We make it possible for people and organizations to transact safely, work productively, and travel freely. Our contactless identification and sensing (RFID, NFC, and BLE) components and solution enabling technologies address the dynamic requirements across multiple industries to wirelessly connect, identify, collect and manage data quickly and accurately across virtually any IoT application.
   

  

**As our Product Manager User Experience, you’ll support HID’s success by:**
* Managing the SW Tools & SDK portfolio and developing it further.
* Providing services and APIs so that the customer can manage our readers within their solutions.
* Being aware of the latest changes in the market (including competitors) when it comes to device management within networks.
* Maintain market and competitive awareness by analyzing market data and applying it to the product strategy, with strategies for monetization.
* Coordination of go-to-market strategies for software tools with marketing communications and sales.
* Close cooperation with Product Owners (PO) and Business Owners (BO) by acting as the voice of the customer.
* Priority balancing between customer needs, market, business case, and internal capabilities.
* Communication with various internal stakeholders, customers, the market, and partners.
* Sales, Pre-Sales, Tech Support, and Professional Services teams training, and all relevant stakeholders in the context of Software Tools & SDK.


**Your Experience and Background include:**
* Bachelor’s degree in engineering
* Candidates with equivalent education plus relevant work experience may also be considered
* Understanding of Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) processes
* Demonstrated ability to work across cultural, geographical, and disciplinary team boundaries
* Modern programming languages such as C, C++, Rest
* Knowledge about cloud architectures and commercial cloud service providers
* Proficient with Jira and Confluence or similar Product Management software
* Proficient in MS Office applications, such as Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Miro etc.
* Familiar with MS Windows, Mac OS, Linux, and mobile OS (Android, iOS)
* Compliance with all relevant HID Global policies and procedures related to Quality, Security, Safety, Business Continuity, and Environmental systems
* Up to 10% of travel and fieldwork, including international travel may be required. Therefore, employee must possess or be able to acquire a valid passport
* Ability to work flexibly to direct and support colleagues located in other time zones


**What we can offer you:**
* Competitive salary and rewards package
* Competitive benefits and annual leave offering, allowing for work-life balance
* A vibrant, welcoming & inclusive culture
* Extensive career development opportunities and resources to maximize your potential
* To be a part of a global organization that is pioneering the hardware, software and services that allow people to confidently navigate the physical and digital worlds


**Why apply?**
* Empowerment: You’ll work as part of a global team in a flexible work environment, learning and enhancing your expertise. We welcome an opportunity to meet you and learn about your unique talents, skills, and experiences. You don’t need to check all the boxes. If you have most of the skills and experience, we want you to apply
* Innovation: You embrace challenges and want to drive change. We are open to ideas, including flexible work arrangements, job sharing or part-time job seekers
* Integrity: You are results-orientated, reliable, and straightforward and value being treated accordingly. We want all our employees to be themselves, to feel appreciated and accepted


 HID is committed to building a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workforce that reflects the global communities we serve. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We evaluate applicants without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristic. Our goal is to create a workplace that empowers everyone to thrive and be their authentic selves, fostering an environment of mutual respect and inclusivity. If you have a disability and require assistance or accommodation to participate in the application process or to perform essential job functions, please contact accommodations-ext@hidglobal.com .
   

  

 We would like to inform you that the ‘Procedure for reporting violations of the law applicable at ASSA ABLOY Opening Solutions Poland S.A company’ has been introduced. , the content of which is available at www.assaabloy.com.pl/pl under ‘Company documents’ and ‘Privacy Center’
   

  

**We make it easier for people to get where they want to go!**
 On an average day, think of how many times you tap, twist, tag, push or swipe to get access, find information, connect with others or track something. HID technology is behind billions of interactions, in more than 100 countries. We help you create a verified, trusted identity that can get you where you need to go – without having to think about it.
   

  

 When you join our HID team, you’ll also be part of the ASSA ABLOY Group, the global leader in access solutions. You’ll have 63,000 colleagues in more than 70 different countries. We empower our people to build their career around their aspirations and our ambitions – supporting them with regular feedback, training, and development opportunities. Our colleagues think broadly about where they can make the most impact, and we encourage them to grow their role locally, regionally, or even internationally. As we welcome new people on board, it’s important to us to have diverse, inclusive teams, and we value different perspectives and experiences.

title: Head of Design
description:
**About The Role**
 As Head of Design at 4C Strategies, you will lead a small, distributed team of designers and strengthen our design capability across product experiences, user workflows, and cross-functional collaboration. This is a hands-on leadership role: you are a skilled designer who both leads the team and keeps doing design work yourself, helping the team do their best work. Based in our Stockholm office, you will play a central role in shaping how design contributes to product strategy, customer value, and delivery across our Exonaut platform — our software for readiness and resilience — and related services. This role focuses on our Defence products, designing for armed forces and defence planners.
   

  

 You will be responsible for building structure, quality, and consistency across the design function — balancing strategic direction with hands-on leadership and operational execution. The role involves close collaboration with Product Management, Engineering, Delivery Consultancy, and customer-facing teams to ensure user-centred, intuitive, and reliable solutions that support mission-critical environments.
   

  

 As Head of Design, you will support and develop designers, strengthen design processes and governance, and ensure alignment between business priorities, customer needs, and product usability. You will help establish design as a key capability within 4C’s continued growth journey.
   

  

**The role at a glance**
* Location: Stockholm, Sweden (hybrid working).
* Travel: mainly regular travel to our Malmö office, plus 2–3 trips abroad per year (for example to our London, Warminster, or Orlando offices).
* Reports to: Chief Technology & Product Officer (CTPO).
* Team: a team of 3 designers, based in Malmö and Orlando (USA).
* Focus: Defence products, designing for armed forces and defence planners.
* Employment type: full-time, permanent (6-month probation).


**Key Responsibilities**
* Work hands-on as a designer on key Defence product areas, contributing your own craft and raising quality together with the team.
* Lead and develop the team through regular 1:1s, coaching, feedback, and clear development plans, creating growth opportunities for each designer.
* Champion design quality, accessibility, and consistency across products, workflows, and customer-facing experiences.
* Improve design processes, design-system standards, and ways of working across cross-functional teams.
* Partner closely with Product Managers and Engineering to align user experience, technical feasibility, and business priorities.
* Turn customer and user insights — from continuous discovery and research — into intuitive, effective products, and measure success through clear outcomes and shared goals.
* Contribute to product strategy, keeping user-centred thinking at the heart of decisions, and set clear design principles for AI-enabled features (transparency, user control, and safe fallback options).
* Recruit and onboard designers, and support their growth through our design career framework, building an inclusive and collaborative team culture.


**About You**
 You are an experienced design leader who combines deep user-centred thinking with structure, collaboration, and business understanding. You are comfortable operating in complex product environments where balancing user needs, technical realities, and organisational priorities is essential.
   

  

 You enjoy building clarity and consistency across teams and processes while supporting designers in their growth and development. You are pragmatic, communicative, and motivated by improving both product experiences and ways of working. You lead by example — both doing design work and growing your team — and you measure your success by your team's quality, cohesion, and growth.
   

  

**We believe you bring**
* Experience leading or coaching designers within SaaS, technology, or product-driven organisations.
* A solid understanding of product design, UX, interaction design, and user-centred methods.
* Experience building and improving scalable design processes, systems, and governance.
* Experience collaborating with Product Management and Engineering teams in cross-functional environments.
* Experience developing designers through coaching, feedback, and regular 1:1s.
* Comfortable leading a distributed team across different locations and time zones.
* Clear communication and stakeholder skills, with the ability to build alignment across teams and organisational levels.
* Ability to balance strategic thinking with operational execution in fast-moving environments.
* Good organisational skills and the ability to manage several projects at once.
* High proficiency in English, both written and verbal.
* Fluent Swedish, both written and spoken.
* Swedish citizenship, because this role supports defence work and the successful candidate must pass Swedish security vetting (säkerhetsprövning).


**Desirable:**
* Experience working with complex B2B platforms or mission-critical software environments.
* Familiarity with design systems and scalable product design practices.
* Experience working in international or multi-disciplinary organisations.
* Experience supporting organisational change and design maturity growth.
* Formal education or training within UX, interaction design, service design, or related disciplines.


 You do not need to tick every box. If you meet most of these points and are excited about the role, we would love to hear from you. A few requirements for this role — such as Swedish citizenship and security vetting — come only from the security rules for defence work, not from personal preference.
   

  

**What We Offer**
* Occupational pension (tjänstepension).
* Wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag).
* Healthcare insurance & healthcare insurance for treatment
* Parental pay
* Vaccinations
* Room to grow into a Director-level design leadership role over time.
* A supportive, collaborative team building meaningful products for mission-critical work.


**About 4C Strategies**
 4C Strategies is a global provider of readiness and resilience solutions, supporting organisations across defence, public sector, corporate, and critical infrastructure environments. Through our Exonaut platform and expert services, we help organisations train, exercise, evaluate, and improve their operational capability and preparedness.
   

  

 At 4C, we combine technology, expertise, and collaboration to solve complex challenges in mission-critical environments. Our teams work closely together across disciplines to create user-focused solutions that strengthen resilience and operational performance worldwide.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 To apply, click the Apply today button. We review applications on a rolling basis, so please apply as soon as you can.

title: Full-Stack Design Technologist
description:
Above is looking for a talented hire (Intermediate/Senior) to join our Design Technology team in either our Malmö or Stockholm studio. This position is a full-time role.
   

  

**The role**
 As an Full-Stack Design Technologist at Above, you will work with multi-disciplinary project teams on a wide variety of cutting-edge design & innovation projects for some of the world's most exciting brands. You will be an integral part of our design team, proactively contributing across the full design process – from research to concept definition, from technical explorations to crafting compelling concept prototypes and strategic narratives.
   

  

 You enjoy working inside design problems – not just implementing solutions. You’re curious about the “why” behind decisions, and you’re motivated to develop taste, critique, and craft over time.
   

  

 Our work spans consumer products, smart home devices, robotics, future mobility experiences, spatial experiences, and more. We create impact across all levels and horizons – from category-defining hero products to multi-dimensional portfolio strategies and shaping compelling future visions alike.
   

  

**How We Work**
 Design Technology is in rapid transition. AI tools accelerate iteration, but they don’t replace the hard parts: judgment, direction-setting, and knowing what’s worth prototyping, validating, and shipping.
   

  

 We value broad tool fluency and curiosity, and using experience prototypes (and other formats when needed) to move projects forward.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Proactively contribute across the design process, from research to concept definition
* Run technical explorations, and craft compelling concept prototypes and strategic narratives
* Shape excellent project deliveries through craft, creativity, and conceptual thinking
* Drive smaller engagements independently, and integrate into larger projects as part of diverse, cross-functional teams
* Act as an ambassador of the design technology discipline within project teams


 What we are looking for
   

  

 We are looking for inspiring, forward-thinking portfolios that display an integrated approach of design, technology, and creativity.
   

  

**Core strengths**
* Preferably 3+ years of professional experience, and familiarity with fast-paced agency environments / workflows
* Forward-thinking approach to technology, with a keen interest in emerging trends and future developments
* Comfortable leading conversations with stakeholders and experts across design, business, and engineering
* Ability to structure ambiguous work and choose the right tools/fidelity at each stage
* Strong craft + conceptual thinking, paired with clear communication and storytelling
* Demonstrated curiosity for design craft (interaction, visual, industrial, or service) – and an appetite to grow it further
* A humble, self-propelled, collaborative team player in multi-competence constellations
* Positive impact and sustainability mindset


**Technical Toolkit (in Order Of Importance)**
*You do not need to tick every box – we’re looking for strong depth in some areas and curiosity to learn the rest.*
* Proven track record in developing interactive systems
* Troubleshooting skills, particularly in bridging design and technical domains
* Knowledge of local and distributed network architectures
* API and integration design
* Modern full-stack: TypeScript, React / Next.js, Node, Python
* Daily fluency with AI coding/prototyping tools: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Weavy, etc.
* AI-native application development: shipping on LLM APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, open models), agent frameworks, tool use, structured generation, streaming/generative UI, writing evals, A/B-ing prompts, model selection, MCP design
* Demonstrated skills and experiences in Game engines (Unity / Unreal)
* Bonus: voice/audio interfaces, computer vision, video editing, physical computing


**A Bit About Above**
 We are a Scandinavian innovation agency dedicated to driving positive change through the integration of design, strategy and technology. Our creative 360-approach allows us to break down boundaries between the physical and digital, while fusing hardware & software into meaningful experiences.
   

  

 We work with leading global brands, local Scandinavian heroes, and a fantastic collection of startups. We’re a team of warm, welcoming, dedicated professionals with diverse, international backgrounds. In everything we do, we strive for a world-class result and believe you share that mindset. You'll join a team that values collaboration, creativity, and driving tangible impact through thoughtful design!
   

  

**Join our team**
 Please submit your portfolio and short motivation statement.
   

  

**Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.**
 If you have any questions regarding the job posting or application process, please send an e-mail to Alexander Pospischil, Director of Design Technology at Above.
   

  

 Interviews with shortlisted candidates will start in August.
   

  

 We are excited to receive international applications, and are generally able to assist with work permit & relocation processes.
   

  

 Let’s build what’s next – thoughtfully, collaboratively, and hands-on.

title: Software Engineer to Rebl Industries
description:
Rebl Industries builds robotic workmates that companies subscribe to instead of buying. Our robots deploy in weeks, learn continuously and take over repetitive, heavy and precision-demanding tasks. With a unique Robot-as-a-Service model, we make automation flexible, scalable and accessible for businesses that want results without the traditional complexity and investment costs of automation projects. We’re a fast-growing, ambitious scale-up working at the intersection of robotics, AI, software and real-world production. Read more about us at www.rebl.industries
  
The logistics industry is undergoing its biggest technological transformation yet. Do you want to be part of it?
  
Join the team shaping the future of warehouse automation
  
At Rebl Industries, we build robotic workmates that companies subscribe to instead of buying. Our robots are deployed within weeks, improve continuously, and take over repetitive, heavy, and precision-demanding tasks. Through our Robot-as-a-Service model, we make automation more flexible, scalable, and accessible for companies looking to modernize without the traditional complexity or large upfront investment.We’re a fast-growing scale-up operating at the intersection of robotics, AI, software, and industrial production. Having recently expanded our engineering team, we are now looking for several new software engineers to continue building the next generation of intelligent automation.
  
What you will do
  
As a Software Engineer at Rebl, you will help build the technical foundation for the next generation of robotic coworkers in warehouse and logistics environments. You will develop core systems and applications in close collaboration with automation engineers and AI specialists.work will span backend, frontend, algorithms, AI models, and integrations with our automation platform. You will contribute to both new development and product evolution, while also supporting deployed solutions as we scale.
  
What we’re looking for
  
We are looking for several developers and strongly believe in the value of combining different competencies, backgrounds, and levels of specialization. Whether you are a specialist or a broad full-stack engineer, you likely bring some of the following:
  
A university degree (BSc or MSc) in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Mechatronics, or a related technical field
  
3–5+ years of professional experience in software engineering within a relevant industry
  
Experience designing, developing, and maintaining backend and/or frontend applications using C#, Python, C++, and TypeScript
  
Solid understanding of CI/CD pipelines in GitHub and application containerization
  
Experience working with digital twin solutions and IoT frameworks
  
Proficiency in developing REST APIs, database structures, and system integrations
  
Experience with 3D engines and visualization applications
  
Familiarity with algorithms and AI modules for motion planning, object recognition, and classification
  
Experience building, training, and integrating AI models such as SAM and SAM2 for vision and automation applications
  
Experience developing annotation tools and training/classification strategies for neural networks
  
Fluency in Swedish, both spoken and written
  
  

Your mindset
  
You have a broad technical mindset and enjoy moving between backend, frontend, and different technologies. You are curious, hands-on, and proactive, and you enjoy turning ideas into working solutions quickly.You take ownership, communicate clearly, and collaborate naturally across disciplines. You thrive in a scaling environment where speed matters, structure evolves, and experimentation is encouraged. Most importantly, you want to build products that create real-world impact and deliver tangible results.
  
Why Rebl Industries
  
At Rebl Industries, you become part of a sharp, ambitious, and supportive team where your work has immediate impact. You help shape the future of robotic automation — not in theory, but through real industrial deployments.As we continue scaling into new markets and applications, your ideas, code, and technical decisions will help define both our product and our future.
  
Apply now
